text: Calculus is based on the concept of a function, which is used to model quantities that depend on one another. For example, it is common to study quantities that change over time. The notion of a limit is also fundamental. We may say that the limit of a function \(f(x)\) is a value \(b\) as \(x\) approaches a value \(a\) , or that \(f(x)\) converges to \(b\) as \(x\) approaches \(a\) . Equivalently, we may say that \(f(x)\) approaches \(b\) as \(x\) approaches a value \(a\) , or that it tends to \(b\) as \(x\) tends to \(a\) . We have already begun to consider such notions in Section 3.6 .
html: Calculus is based on the concept of a function, which is used to model quantities that depend on one another. For example, it is common to study quantities that change over time. The notion of a limit is also fundamental. We may say that the limit of a function \(f(x)\) is a value \(b\) as \(x\) approaches a value \(a\), or that \(f(x)\) converges to \(b\) as \(x\) approaches \(a\). Equivalently, we may say that \(f(x)\) approaches \(b\) as \(x\) approaches a value \(a\), or that it tends to \(b\) as \(x\) tends to \(a\). We have already begun to consider such notions in Section 3.6.
zh: 微积分建立在函数概念之上，函数用于刻画相互依赖的量。例如，研究随时间变化的量是很常见的。极限概念同样是基础性的。我们可以说函数 \(f(x)\) 在 \(x\) 趋近于 \(a\) 时的极限为 \(b\)，也可以说当 \(x\) 趋近于 \(a\) 时，\(f(x)\) 收敛到 \(b\)。等价地，可以说当 \(x\) 趋近于 \(a\) 时，\(f(x)\) 趋近于 \(b\)；或说当 \(x\) 趋向于 \(a\) 时，它趋向 \(b\)。我们已经在Section 3.6中开始考察这类概念。

text: Topology is the abstract study of limits and continuity. Having covered the essentials of formalization in Chapters 2 to 7 , in this chapter, we will explain how topological notions are formalized in Mathlib. Not only do topological abstractions apply in much greater generality, but they also, somewhat paradoxically, make it easier to reason about limits and continuity in concrete instances.
html: Topology is the abstract study of limits and continuity. Having covered the essentials of formalization in Chapters 2 to 7, in this chapter, we will explain how topological notions are formalized in Mathlib. Not only do topological abstractions apply in much greater generality, but they also, somewhat paradoxically, make it easier to reason about limits and continuity in concrete instances.
zh: 拓扑学是对极限与连续性的抽象研究。在第 2 章至第 7 章介绍完形式化的基本内容之后，本章将解释 Mathlib 中如何形式化拓扑概念。拓扑抽象不仅适用于大得多的一般性范围，而且有些反直觉的是，它还使我们在具体实例中更容易推理极限和连续性。

text: Topological notions build on quite a few layers of mathematical structure. The first layer is naive set theory, as described in Chapter 4 . The next layer is the theory of filters , which we will describe in Section 11.1 . On top of that, we layer the theories of topological spaces , metric spaces , and a slightly more exotic intermediate notion called a uniform space .
html: Topological notions build on quite a few layers of mathematical structure. The first layer is naive set theory, as described in Chapter 4. The next layer is the theory of filters, which we will describe in Section 11.1. On top of that, we layer the theories of topological spaces, metric spaces, and a slightly more exotic intermediate notion called a uniform space.
zh: 拓扑概念建立在若干层数学结构之上。第一层是朴素集合论，如Chapter 4所述。下一层是滤子理论，我们将在Section 11.1中介绍。在此之上，还叠加了拓扑空间、度量空间以及一个稍显特殊的中间概念，称为一致空间。

text: Whereas previous chapters relied on mathematical notions that were likely familiar to you, the notion of a filter is less well known, even to many working mathematicians. The notion is essential, however, for formalizing mathematics effectively. Let us explain why. Let f : ℝ → ℝ be any function. We can consider the limit of f x as x approaches some value x₀ , but we can also consider the limit of f x as x approaches infinity or negative infinity. We can moreover consider the limit of f x as x approaches x₀ from the right, conventionally written x₀⁺ , or from the left, written x₀⁻ . There are variations where x approaches x₀ or x₀⁺ or x₀⁻ but is not allowed to take on the value x₀ itself. This results in at least eight ways that x can approach something. We can also restrict to rational values of x or place other constraints on the domain, but let’s stick to those 8 cases.
html: Whereas previous chapters relied on mathematical notions that were likely familiar to you, the notion of a filter is less well known, even to many working mathematicians. The notion is essential, however, for formalizing mathematics effectively. Let us explain why. Let f : ℝ → ℝ be any function. We can consider the limit of f x as x approaches some value x₀, but we can also consider the limit of f x as x approaches infinity or negative infinity. We can moreover consider the limit of f x as x approaches x₀ from the right, conventionally written x₀⁺, or from the left, written x₀⁻. There are variations where x approaches x₀ or x₀⁺ or x₀⁻ but is not allowed to take on the value x₀ itself. This results in at least eight ways that x can approach something. We can also restrict to rational values of x or place other constraints on the domain, but let’s stick to those 8 cases.
zh: 前几章依赖的数学概念大多可能为你所熟悉；相比之下，滤子概念即使对许多职业数学家也不那么常见。然而，为了有效形式化数学，这一概念是必不可少的。下面说明原因。设 f : ℝ → ℝ 为任意函数。我们可以考虑当 x 趋近于某个值 x₀ 时 f x 的极限，也可以考虑当 x 趋向正无穷或负无穷时 f x 的极限。此外，还可以考虑当 x 从右侧趋近于 x₀（惯常写作 x₀⁺）或从左侧趋近于 x₀⁻ 时 f x 的极限。还有一些变体，其中 x 趋近于 x₀、x₀⁺ 或 x₀⁻，但不允许取值为 x₀ 本身。这样，x 趋近某物的方式至少有八种。还可以把 x 限制为有理数，或对定义域施加其他约束；但这里先只考虑这 8 种情形。

text: We have a similar variety of options on the codomain: we can specify that f x approaches a value from the left or right, or that it approaches positive or negative infinity, and so on. For example, we may wish to say that f x tends to +∞ when x tends to x₀ from the right without being equal to x₀ . This results in 64 different kinds of limit statements, and we haven’t even begun to deal with limits of sequences, as we did in Section 3.6 .
html: We have a similar variety of options on the codomain: we can specify that f x approaches a value from the left or right, or that it approaches positive or negative infinity, and so on. For example, we may wish to say that f x tends to +∞ when x tends to x₀ from the right without being equal to x₀. This results in 64 different kinds of limit statements, and we haven’t even begun to deal with limits of sequences, as we did in Section 3.6.
zh: 在余定义域上也有类似的多种选择：可以指定 f x 从左侧或右侧趋近某个值，或者趋向正无穷或负无穷，等等。例如，可能想表达：当 x 从右侧趋近 x₀ 且不等于 x₀ 时，f x 趋向 +∞。这会产生 64 种不同的极限陈述，而我们甚至还没有开始处理序列极限，后者曾在Section 3.6中出现。

text: The problem is compounded even further when it comes to the supporting lemmas. For instance, limits compose: if f x tends to y₀ when x tends to x₀ and g y tends to z₀ when y tends to y₀ then g ∘ f x tends to z₀ when x tends to x₀ . There are three notions of “tends to” at play here, each of which can be instantiated in any of the eight ways described in the previous paragraph. This results in 512 lemmas, a lot to have to add to a library! Informally, mathematicians generally prove two or three of these and simply note that the rest can be proved “in the same way.” Formalizing mathematics requires making the relevant notion of “sameness” fully explicit, and that is exactly what Bourbaki’s theory of filters manages to do.
html: The problem is compounded even further when it comes to the supporting lemmas. For instance, limits compose: if f x tends to y₀ when x tends to x₀ and g y tends to z₀ when y tends to y₀ then g ∘ f x tends to z₀ when x tends to x₀. There are three notions of “tends to” at play here, each of which can be instantiated in any of the eight ways described in the previous paragraph. This results in 512 lemmas, a lot to have to add to a library! Informally, mathematicians generally prove two or three of these and simply note that the rest can be proved “in the same way.” Formalizing mathematics requires making the relevant notion of “sameness” fully explicit, and that is exactly what Bourbaki’s theory of filters manages to do.
zh: 涉及支撑引理时，问题会进一步复杂化。例如，极限可以复合：若当 x 趋向 x₀ 时 f x 趋向 y₀，且当 y 趋向 y₀ 时 g y 趋向 z₀，则当 x 趋向 x₀ 时 g ∘ f x 趋向 z₀。这里有三个“趋向”概念，每一个都可以按前一段所述的八种方式实例化。于是得到 512 个引理；若都加入库中，数量相当庞大。非形式化地说，数学家通常证明其中两三个，然后只说明其余可以“同样地”证明。形式化数学要求把相关的“相同方式”完全明确化，而这正是布尔巴基的滤子理论所做到的。

text: A filter on a type X is a collection of sets of X that satisfies three conditions that we will spell out below. The notion supports two related ideas:
html: A filter on a type X is a collection of sets of X that satisfies three conditions that we will spell out below. The notion supports two related ideas:
zh: 类型 X 上的滤子是 X 的若干集合组成的族，并满足下面将列出的三个条件。这个概念支撑两类相关想法：

text: The filters that correspond to these descriptions will be defined later in this section, but we can already name them:
html: The filters that correspond to these descriptions will be defined later in this section, but we can already name them:
zh: 与这些描述对应的滤子将在本节稍后定义，但我们已经可以先给出它们的名称：

text: The general definition is as follows: a filter F : Filter X is a collection of sets F.sets : Set (Set X) satisfying the following:
html: The general definition is as follows: a filter F : Filter X is a collection of sets F.sets : Set (Set X) satisfying the following:
zh: 一般定义如下：滤子 F : Filter X 是由若干集合组成的族 F.sets : Set (Set X)，并满足下列条件：

text: The first condition says that the set of all elements of X belongs to F.sets . The second condition says that if U belongs to F.sets then anything containing U also belongs to F.sets . The third condition says that F.sets is closed under finite intersections. In Mathlib, a filter F is defined to be a structure bundling F.sets and its three properties, but the properties carry no additional data, and it is convenient to blur the distinction between F and F.sets . We therefore define U ∈ F to mean U ∈ F.sets . This explains why the word sets appears in the names of some lemmas that that mention U ∈ F .
html: The first condition says that the set of all elements of X belongs to F.sets. The second condition says that if U belongs to F.sets then anything containing U also belongs to F.sets. The third condition says that F.sets is closed under finite intersections. In Mathlib, a filter F is defined to be a structure bundling F.sets and its three properties, but the properties carry no additional data, and it is convenient to blur the distinction between F and F.sets. We therefore define U ∈ F to mean U ∈ F.sets. This explains why the word sets appears in the names of some lemmas that that mention U ∈ F.
zh: 第一个条件说，X 的全体元素组成的集合属于 F.sets。第二个条件说，若 U 属于 F.sets，则任何包含 U 的集合也属于 F.sets。第三个条件说，F.sets 在有限交下封闭。在 Mathlib 中，滤子 F 被定义为一个打包了 F.sets 及其三个性质的结构；但这些性质不携带额外数据，因此混同 F 与 F.sets 很方便。于是我们把 U ∈ F 定义为 U ∈ F.sets。这解释了为什么有些提及 U ∈ F 的引理名称中会出现 sets 一词。

text: It may help to think of a filter as defining a notion of a “sufficiently large” set. The first condition then says that univ is sufficiently large, the second one says that a set containing a sufficiently large set is sufficiently large and the third one says that the intersection of two sufficiently large sets is sufficiently large.
html: It may help to think of a filter as defining a notion of a “sufficiently large” set. The first condition then says that univ is sufficiently large, the second one says that a set containing a sufficiently large set is sufficiently large and the third one says that the intersection of two sufficiently large sets is sufficiently large.
zh: 把滤子理解为定义了“充分大”集合的概念，可能会有所帮助。第一个条件于是说 univ 是充分大的；第二个条件说，包含充分大集合的集合仍然充分大；第三个条件说，两个充分大集合的交仍然充分大。

text: It may be even more useful to think of a filter on a type X as a generalized element of Set X . For instance, atTop is the “set of very large numbers” and 𝓝 x₀ is the “set of points very close to x₀ .” One manifestation of this view is that we can associate to any s : Set X the so-called principal filter consisting of all sets that contain s . This definition is already in Mathlib and has a notation 𝓟 (localized in the Filter namespace). For the purpose of demonstration, we ask you to take this opportunity to work out the definition here.
html: It may be even more useful to think of a filter on a type X as a generalized element of Set X. For instance, atTop is the “set of very large numbers” and 𝓝 x₀ is the “set of points very close to x₀.” One manifestation of this view is that we can associate to any s : Set X the so-called principal filter consisting of all sets that contain s. This definition is already in Mathlib and has a notation 𝓟 (localized in the Filter namespace). For the purpose of demonstration, we ask you to take this opportunity to work out the definition here.
zh: 更有用的想法可能是，把类型 X 上的滤子视为 Set X 的广义元素。例如，atTop 是“非常大的数的集合”，而 𝓝 x₀ 是“非常接近 x₀ 的点的集合”。这种观点的一个体现是，可以把任意 s : Set X 关联到所谓的主滤子，它由所有包含 s 的集合组成。这个定义已在 Mathlib 中，并有记号 𝓟（局部化在 Filter 命名空间中）。为了演示，我们请你借此机会在这里完成这个定义。

text: For our second example, we ask you to define the filter atTop : Filter ℕ . (We could use any type with a preorder instead of ℕ .)
html: For our second example, we ask you to define the filter atTop : Filter ℕ. (We could use any type with a preorder instead of ℕ.)
zh: 作为第二个例子，我们请你定义滤子 atTop : Filter ℕ。（也可以用任何带预序的类型替代 ℕ。）

text: We can also directly define the filter 𝓝 x of neighborhoods of any x : ℝ . In the real numbers, a neighborhood of x is a set containing an open interval \((x_0 - \varepsilon, x_0 + \varepsilon)\) , defined in Mathlib as Ioo (x₀ - ε) (x₀ + ε) . (This notion of a neighborhood is only a special case of a more general construction in Mathlib.)
html: We can also directly define the filter 𝓝 x of neighborhoods of any x : ℝ. In the real numbers, a neighborhood of x is a set containing an open interval \((x_0 - \varepsilon, x_0 + \varepsilon)\), defined in Mathlib as Ioo (x₀ - ε) (x₀ + ε). (This notion of a neighborhood is only a special case of a more general construction in Mathlib.)
zh: 还可以直接定义任意 x : ℝ 的邻域滤子 𝓝 x。在实数中，x 的一个邻域是包含开区间 \((x_0 - \varepsilon, x_0 + \varepsilon)\) 的集合；该开区间在 Mathlib 中定义为 Ioo (x₀ - ε) (x₀ + ε)。（这种邻域概念只是 Mathlib 中更一般构造的一个特例。）

text: With these examples, we can already define what it means for a function f : X → Y to converge to some G : Filter Y along some F : Filter X , as follows:
html: With these examples, we can already define what it means for a function f : X → Y to converge to some G : Filter Y along some F : Filter X, as follows:
zh: 有了这些例子，已经可以如下定义函数 f : X → Y 沿某个 F : Filter X 收敛到某个 G : Filter Y 的含义：

text: When X is ℕ and Y is ℝ , Tendsto₁ u atTop (𝓝 x) is equivalent to saying that the sequence u : ℕ → ℝ converges to the real number x . When both X and Y are ℝ , Tendsto f (𝓝 x₀) (𝓝 y₀) is equivalent to the familiar notion \(\lim_{x \to x₀} f(x) = y₀\) . All of the other kinds of limits mentioned in the introduction are also equivalent to instances of Tendsto₁ for suitable choices of filters on the source and target.
html: When X is ℕ and Y is ℝ, Tendsto₁ u atTop (𝓝 x) is equivalent to saying that the sequence u : ℕ → ℝ converges to the real number x. When both X and Y are ℝ, Tendsto f (𝓝 x₀) (𝓝 y₀) is equivalent to the familiar notion \(\lim_{x \to x₀} f(x) = y₀\). All of the other kinds of limits mentioned in the introduction are also equivalent to instances of Tendsto₁ for suitable choices of filters on the source and target.
zh: 当 X 为 ℕ 且 Y 为 ℝ 时，Tendsto₁ u atTop (𝓝 x) 等价于说序列 u : ℕ → ℝ 收敛到实数 x。当 X 与 Y 都是 ℝ 时，Tendsto f (𝓝 x₀) (𝓝 y₀) 等价于熟悉的概念 \(\lim_{x \to x₀} f(x) = y₀\)。引言中提到的所有其他极限类型，也都等价于对源和目标上的滤子作适当选择后得到的 Tendsto₁ 实例。

text: The notion Tendsto₁ above is definitionally equivalent to the notion Tendsto that is defined in Mathlib, but the latter is defined more abstractly. The problem with the definition of Tendsto₁ is that it exposes a quantifier and elements of G , and it hides the intuition that we get by viewing filters as generalized sets. We can hide the quantifier ∀ V and make the intuition more salient by using more algebraic and set-theoretic machinery. The first ingredient is the pushforward operation \(f_*\) associated to any map f : X → Y , denoted Filter.map f in Mathlib. Given a filter F on X , Filter.map f F : Filter Y is defined so that V ∈ Filter.map f F ↔ f ⁻¹' V ∈ F holds definitionally. In the example file we’ve opened the Filter namespace so that Filter.map can be written as map . This means that we can rewrite the definition of Tendsto using the order relation on Filter Y , which is reversed inclusion of the set of members. In other words, given G H : Filter Y , we have G ≤ H ↔ ∀ V : Set Y, V ∈ H → V ∈ G .
html: The notion Tendsto₁ above is definitionally equivalent to the notion Tendsto that is defined in Mathlib, but the latter is defined more abstractly. The problem with the definition of Tendsto₁ is that it exposes a quantifier and elements of G, and it hides the intuition that we get by viewing filters as generalized sets. We can hide the quantifier ∀ V and make the intuition more salient by using more algebraic and set-theoretic machinery. The first ingredient is the pushforward operation \(f_*\) associated to any map f : X → Y, denoted Filter.map f in Mathlib. Given a filter F on X, Filter.map f F : Filter Y is defined so that V ∈ Filter.map f F ↔ f ⁻¹' V ∈ F holds definitionally. In the example file we’ve opened the Filter namespace so that Filter.map can be written as map. This means that we can rewrite the definition of Tendsto using the order relation on Filter Y, which is reversed inclusion of the set of members. In other words, given G H : Filter Y, we have G ≤ H ↔ ∀ V : Set Y, V ∈ H → V ∈ G.
zh: 上面的概念 Tendsto₁ 与 Mathlib 中定义的 Tendsto 按定义等价，但后者定义得更加抽象。Tendsto₁ 的定义问题在于，它显式暴露了量词以及 G 的元素，并隐藏了把滤子看作广义集合时得到的直观。通过使用更多代数和集合论机制，可以隐藏量词 ∀ V，并让直观更突出。第一个成分是与任意映射 f : X → Y 对应的pushforward操作 \(f_*\)，在 Mathlib 中记作 Filter.map f。给定 X 上的滤子 F，Filter.map f F : Filter Y 被定义为使 V ∈ Filter.map f F ↔ f ⁻¹' V ∈ F 按定义成立。在示例文件中，我们打开了 Filter 命名空间，因此 Filter.map 可以写作 map。这意味着可以用 Filter Y 上的序关系重写 Tendsto 的定义；这个序关系是成员集合包含关系的反向。换言之，给定 G H : Filter Y，有 G ≤ H ↔ ∀ V : Set Y, V ∈ H → V ∈ G。

text: It may seem that the order relation on filters is backward. But recall that we can view filters on X as generalized elements of Set X , via the inclusion of 𝓟 : Set X → Filter X which maps any set s to the corresponding principal filter. This inclusion is order preserving, so the order relation on Filter can indeed be seen as the natural inclusion relation between generalized sets. In this analogy, pushforward is analogous to the direct image. And, indeed, map f (𝓟 s) = 𝓟 (f '' s) .
html: It may seem that the order relation on filters is backward. But recall that we can view filters on X as generalized elements of Set X, via the inclusion of 𝓟 : Set X → Filter X which maps any set s to the corresponding principal filter. This inclusion is order preserving, so the order relation on Filter can indeed be seen as the natural inclusion relation between generalized sets. In this analogy, pushforward is analogous to the direct image. And, indeed, map f (𝓟 s) = 𝓟 (f '' s).
zh: 滤子上的序关系看起来也许是反的。但请回忆，可以通过嵌入 𝓟 : Set X → Filter X 把 X 上的滤子看成 Set X 的广义元素；该嵌入把任意集合 s 送到相应的主滤子。这个嵌入保持序，因此 Filter 上的序关系确实可以视为广义集合之间的自然包含关系。在这个类比中，推出类似于直接像。事实上，map f (𝓟 s) = 𝓟 (f '' s)。

text: We can now understand intuitively why a sequence u : ℕ → ℝ converges to a point x₀ if and only if we have map u atTop ≤ 𝓝 x₀ . The inequality means the “direct image under u ” of “the set of very big natural numbers” is “included” in “the set of points very close to x₀ .”
html: We can now understand intuitively why a sequence u : ℕ → ℝ converges to a point x₀ if and only if we have map u atTop ≤ 𝓝 x₀. The inequality means the “direct image under u” of “the set of very big natural numbers” is “included” in “the set of points very close to x₀.”
zh: 现在可以直观地理解，为什么序列 u : ℕ → ℝ 收敛到点 x₀ 当且仅当有 map u atTop ≤ 𝓝 x₀。这个不等式表示，“非常大的自然数集合”在 u 下的“直接像”被“包含”在“非常接近 x₀ 的点的集合”中。

text: As promised, the definition of Tendsto₂ does not exhibit any quantifiers or sets. It also leverages the algebraic properties of the pushforward operation. First, each Filter.map f is monotone. And, second, Filter.map is compatible with composition.
html: As promised, the definition of Tendsto₂ does not exhibit any quantifiers or sets. It also leverages the algebraic properties of the pushforward operation. First, each Filter.map f is monotone. And, second, Filter.map is compatible with composition.
zh: 如所承诺的，Tendsto₂ 的定义不再显式出现量词或集合。它还利用了推出操作的代数性质。首先，每个 Filter.map f 都是单调的。其次，Filter.map 与复合相容。

text: Together these two properties allow us to prove that limits compose, yielding in one shot all 512 variants of the composition lemma described in the introduction, and lots more. You can practice proving the following statement using either the definition of Tendsto₁ in terms of the universal quantifier or the algebraic definition, together with the two lemmas above.

You can practice proving the following statement using either the definition of Tendsto₁ in terms of the universal quantifier or the algebraic definition, together with the two lemmas above.
zh: 这两个性质合在一起，使我们能够证明极限的复合性；它一次性给出引言中所述复合引理的全部 512 个变体，以及更多结果。你可以练习证明下面的陈述：既可以使用以全称量词表述的 Tendsto₁ 定义，也可以使用代数定义，并结合上面的两个引理。

text: The pushforward construction uses a map to push filters from the map source to the map target. There also a pullback operation, Filter.comap , going in the other direction. This generalizes the preimage operation on sets. For any map f , Filter.map f and Filter.comap f form what is known as a Galois connection , which is to say, they satisfy

This generalizes the preimage operation on sets. For any map f, Filter.map f and Filter.comap f form what is known as a Galois connection, which is to say, they satisfy

text: Filter.map_le_iff_le_comap : Filter.map f F ≤ G ↔ F ≤ Filter.comap f G

zh: 即 Filter.map_le_iff_le_comap : Filter.map f F ≤ G ↔ F ≤ Filter.comap f G

text: for every F and G . This operation could be used to provided another formulation of Tendsto that would be provably (but not definitionally) equivalent to the one in Mathlib.
html: for every F and G. This operation could be used to provided another formulation of Tendsto that would be provably (but not definitionally) equivalent to the one in Mathlib.
zh: 对每个 F 和 G 都成立。这个操作可以用于给出 Tendsto 的另一种表述；它与 Mathlib 中的定义可证明等价，但不按定义等价。

text: The comap operation can be used to restrict filters to a subtype. For instance, suppose we have f : ℝ → ℝ , x₀ : ℝ and y₀ : ℝ , and suppose we want to state that f x approaches y₀ when x approaches x₀ within the rational numbers. We can pull the filter 𝓝 x₀ back to ℚ using the coercion map (↑) : ℚ → ℝ and state Tendsto (f ∘ (↑) : ℚ → ℝ) (comap (↑) (𝓝 x₀)) (𝓝 y₀) .
html: The comap operation can be used to restrict filters to a subtype. For instance, suppose we have f : ℝ → ℝ, x₀ : ℝ and y₀ : ℝ, and suppose we want to state that f x approaches y₀ when x approaches x₀ within the rational numbers. We can pull the filter 𝓝 x₀ back to ℚ using the coercion map (↑) : ℚ → ℝ and state Tendsto (f ∘ (↑) : ℚ → ℝ) (comap (↑) (𝓝 x₀)) (𝓝 y₀).
zh: comap 操作可以用来把滤子限制到子类型上。例如，设有 f : ℝ → ℝ、x₀ : ℝ 和 y₀ : ℝ，并且想陈述当 x 在有理数内趋近于 x₀ 时，f x 趋近于 y₀。可以使用强制映射 (↑) : ℚ → ℝ，把滤子 𝓝 x₀ 拉回到 ℚ，并陈述 Tendsto (f ∘ (↑) : ℚ → ℝ) (comap (↑) (𝓝 x₀)) (𝓝 y₀)。

text: The pullback operation is also compatible with composition, but it is contravariant , which is to say, it reverses the order of the arguments.
html: The pullback operation is also compatible with composition, but it is contravariant, which is to say, it reverses the order of the arguments.
zh: 拉回操作也与复合相容，但它是反变的，也就是说，它会反转参数的顺序。

text: Let’s now shift attention to the plane ℝ × ℝ and try to understand how the neighborhoods of a point (x₀, y₀) are related to 𝓝 x₀ and 𝓝 y₀ . There is a product operation Filter.prod : Filter X → Filter Y → Filter (X × Y) , denoted by ×ˢ , which answers this question:

zh: 现在把注意力转向平面 ℝ × ℝ，并尝试理解点 (x₀, y₀) 的邻域与 𝓝 x₀、𝓝 y₀ 之间的关系。有一个积操作 Filter.prod : Filter X → Filter Y → Filter (X × Y)，记作 ×ˢ，它回答了这个问题：

text: The product operation is defined in terms of the pullback operation and the inf operation:
html: The product operation is defined in terms of the pullback operation and the inf operation:

text: F ×ˢ G = (comap Prod.fst F) ⊓ (comap Prod.snd G) .
html: F ×ˢ G = (comap Prod.fst F) ⊓ (comap Prod.snd G).
zh: 即 F ×ˢ G = (comap Prod.fst F) ⊓ (comap Prod.snd G)。

text: Here the inf operation refers to the lattice structure on Filter X for any type X , whereby F ⊓ G is the greatest filter that is smaller than both F and G . Thus the inf operation generalizes the notion of the intersection of sets.
html: Here the inf operation refers to the lattice structure on Filter X for any type X, whereby F ⊓ G is the greatest filter that is smaller than both F and G. Thus the inf operation generalizes the notion of the intersection of sets.
zh: 这里的 inf 运算指的是任意类型 X 上 Filter X 的格结构；其中 F ⊓ G 是同时小于 F 和 G 的最大滤子。因此，inf 运算推广了集合交的概念。

text: A lot of proofs in Mathlib use all of the aforementioned structure ( map , comap , inf , sup , and prod ) to give algebraic proofs about convergence without ever referring to members of filters. You can practice doing this in a proof of the following lemma, unfolding the definition of Tendsto and Filter.prod if needed.
html: A lot of proofs in Mathlib use all of the aforementioned structure (map, comap, inf, sup, and prod) to give algebraic proofs about convergence without ever referring to members of filters. You can practice doing this in a proof of the following lemma, unfolding the definition of Tendsto and Filter.prod if needed.
zh: Mathlib 中大量证明会使用上述全部结构（map、comap、inf、sup 和 prod），给出关于收敛性的代数证明，而从不提及滤子的成员。你可以在证明下面引理时练习这一点；必要时可展开 Tendsto 与 Filter.prod 的定义。

text: The ordered type Filter X is actually a complete lattice, which is to say, there is a bottom element, there is a top element, and every set of filters on X has an Inf and a Sup .
html: The ordered type Filter X is actually a complete lattice, which is to say, there is a bottom element, there is a top element, and every set of filters on X has an Inf and a Sup.
zh: 有序类型 Filter X 实际上是一个完备格，也就是说，它有底元和顶元，并且 X 上任意滤子集合都有一个 Inf 和一个 Sup。

text: Note that given the second property in the definition of a filter (if U belongs to F then anything larger than U also belongs to F ), the first property (the set of all inhabitants of X belongs to F ) is equivalent to the property that F is not the empty collection of sets. This shouldn’t be confused with the more subtle question as to whether the empty set is an element of F . The definition of a filter does not prohibit ∅ ∈ F , but if the empty set is in F then every set is in F , which is to say, ∀ U : Set X, U ∈ F . In this case, F is a rather trivial filter, which is precisely the bottom element of the complete lattice Filter X . This contrasts with the definition of filters in Bourbaki, which doesn’t allow filters containing the empty set.
html: Note that given the second property in the definition of a filter (if U belongs to F then anything larger than U also belongs to F), the first property (the set of all inhabitants of X belongs to F) is equivalent to the property that F is not the empty collection of sets. This shouldn’t be confused with the more subtle question as to whether the empty set is an element of F. The definition of a filter does not prohibit ∅ ∈ F, but if the empty set is in F then every set is in F, which is to say, ∀ U : Set X, U ∈ F. In this case, F is a rather trivial filter, which is precisely the bottom element of the complete lattice Filter X. This contrasts with the definition of filters in Bourbaki, which doesn’t allow filters containing the empty set.
zh: 注意，给定滤子定义中的第二个性质（若 U 属于 F，则任何比 U 更大的集合也属于 F），第一个性质（X 的所有元素组成的集合属于 F）等价于 F 不是空的集合族。不要把这一点与“空集是否为 F 的元素”这一更微妙的问题混淆。滤子的定义并不禁止 ∅ ∈ F；但如果空集在 F 中，那么每个集合都在 F 中，也就是说 ∀ U : Set X, U ∈ F。此时，F 是一个相当平凡的滤子，正是完备格 Filter X 的底元。这与布尔巴基的滤子定义不同，后者不允许滤子包含空集。

text: Because we include the trivial filter in our definition, we sometimes need to explicitly assume nontriviality in some lemmas. In return, however, the theory has nicer global properties. We have already seen that including the trivial filter gives us a bottom element. It also allows us to define principal : Set X → Filter X , which maps ∅ to ⊥ , without adding a precondition to rule out the empty set. And it allows us to define the pullback operation without a precondition as well. Indeed, it can happen that comap f F = ⊥ although F ≠ ⊥ . For instance, given x₀ : ℝ and s : Set ℝ , the pullback of 𝓝 x₀ under the coercion from the subtype corresponding to s is nontrivial if and only if x₀ belongs to the closure of s .
html: Because we include the trivial filter in our definition, we sometimes need to explicitly assume nontriviality in some lemmas. In return, however, the theory has nicer global properties. We have already seen that including the trivial filter gives us a bottom element. It also allows us to define principal : Set X → Filter X, which maps ∅ to ⊥, without adding a precondition to rule out the empty set. And it allows us to define the pullback operation without a precondition as well. Indeed, it can happen that comap f F = ⊥ although F ≠ ⊥. For instance, given x₀ : ℝ and s : Set ℝ, the pullback of 𝓝 x₀ under the coercion from the subtype corresponding to s is nontrivial if and only if x₀ belongs to the closure of s.
zh: 因为我们的定义包含平凡滤子，有些引理中就需要显式假设非平凡性。不过作为回报，理论具有更好的整体性质。我们已经看到，包含平凡滤子给出了底元。它还允许定义 principal : Set X → Filter X，把 ∅ 映到 ⊥，而无需添加排除空集的前提。它也同样允许无前提地定义拉回操作。事实上，可能出现 comap f F = ⊥ 但 F ≠ ⊥ 的情况。例如，给定 x₀ : ℝ 和 s : Set ℝ，在与 s 对应的子类型到实数的强制映射下，𝓝 x₀ 的拉回非平凡，当且仅当 x₀ 属于 s 的闭包。

text: In order to manage lemmas that do need to assume some filter is nontrivial, Mathlib has a type class Filter.NeBot , and the library has lemmas that assume (F : Filter X) [F.NeBot] . The instance database knows, for example, that (atTop : Filter ℕ).NeBot , and it knows that pushing forward a nontrivial filter gives a nontrivial filter. As a result, a lemma assuming [F.NeBot] will automatically apply to map u atTop for any sequence u .
html: In order to manage lemmas that do need to assume some filter is nontrivial, Mathlib has a type class Filter.NeBot, and the library has lemmas that assume (F : Filter X) [F.NeBot]. The instance database knows, for example, that (atTop : Filter ℕ).NeBot, and it knows that pushing forward a nontrivial filter gives a nontrivial filter. As a result, a lemma assuming [F.NeBot] will automatically apply to map u atTop for any sequence u.
zh: 为了管理那些确实需要假设某个滤子非平凡的引理，Mathlib 有一个类型类 Filter.NeBot，库中也有假设 (F : Filter X) [F.NeBot] 的引理。例如，实例数据库知道 (atTop : Filter ℕ).NeBot，并且知道非平凡滤子的推出仍为非平凡滤子。因此，一个假设 [F.NeBot] 的引理会自动适用于任意序列 u 的 map u atTop。

text: Our tour of the algebraic properties of filters and their relation to limits is essentially done, but we have not yet justified our claim to have recaptured the usual limit notions. Superficially, it may seem that Tendsto u atTop (𝓝 x₀) is stronger than the notion of convergence defined in Section 3.6 because we ask that every neighborhood of x₀ has a preimage belonging to atTop , whereas the usual definition only requires this for the standard neighborhoods Ioo (x₀ - ε) (x₀ + ε) . The key is that, by definition, every neighborhood contains such a standard one. This observation leads to the notion of a filter basis .
html: Our tour of the algebraic properties of filters and their relation to limits is essentially done, but we have not yet justified our claim to have recaptured the usual limit notions. Superficially, it may seem that Tendsto u atTop (𝓝 x₀) is stronger than the notion of convergence defined in Section 3.6 because we ask that every neighborhood of x₀ has a preimage belonging to atTop, whereas the usual definition only requires this for the standard neighborhoods Ioo (x₀ - ε) (x₀ + ε). The key is that, by definition, every neighborhood contains such a standard one. This observation leads to the notion of a filter basis.
zh: 我们对滤子的代数性质及其与极限关系的巡览基本完成了，但还没有说明为什么这确实重新获得了通常的极限概念。表面上看，Tendsto u atTop (𝓝 x₀) 似乎强于Section 3.6中定义的收敛概念，因为这里要求 x₀ 的每个邻域的原像都属于 atTop，而通常定义只要求标准邻域 Ioo (x₀ - ε) (x₀ + ε) 满足这一点。关键在于，按定义，每个邻域都包含这样一个标准邻域。这个观察引出滤子基的概念。

text: Given F : Filter X , a family of sets s : ι → Set X is a basis for F if for every set U , we have U ∈ F if and only if it contains some s i . In other words, formally speaking, s is a basis if it satisfies ∀ U : Set X, U ∈ F ↔ ∃ i, s i ⊆ U . It is even more flexible to consider a predicate on ι that selects only some of the values i in the indexing type. In the case of 𝓝 x₀ , we want ι to be ℝ , we write ε for i , and the predicate should select the positive values of ε . So the fact that the sets Ioo (x₀ - ε) (x₀ + ε) form a basis for the neighborhood topology on ℝ is stated as follows:
html: Given F : Filter X, a family of sets s : ι → Set X is a basis for F if for every set U, we have U ∈ F if and only if it contains some s i. In other words, formally speaking, s is a basis if it satisfies ∀ U : Set X, U ∈ F ↔ ∃ i, s i ⊆ U. It is even more flexible to consider a predicate on ι that selects only some of the values i in the indexing type. In the case of 𝓝 x₀, we want ι to be ℝ, we write ε for i, and the predicate should select the positive values of ε. So the fact that the sets Ioo  (x₀ - ε) (x₀ + ε) form a basis for the neighborhood topology on ℝ is stated as follows:
zh: 给定 F : Filter X，若集合族 s : ι → Set X 满足：对每个集合 U，都有 U ∈ F 当且仅当它包含某个 s i，则称它是 F 的基。换言之，形式上说，s 是基当且仅当它满足 ∀ U : Set X, U ∈ F ↔ ∃ i, s i ⊆ U。更灵活的做法是在 ι 上考虑一个谓词，只选择指标类型中的某些值 i。在 𝓝 x₀ 的情形中，我们希望 ι 是 ℝ，把 i 写作 ε，并且该谓词应当选出正的 ε。因此，集合 Ioo  (x₀ - ε) (x₀ + ε) 构成 ℝ 上邻域拓扑的基这一事实，陈述如下：

text: There is also a nice basis for the filter atTop . The lemma Filter.HasBasis.tendsto_iff allows us to reformulate a statement of the form Tendsto f F G given bases for F and G . Putting these pieces together gives us essentially the notion of convergence that we used in Section 3.6 .
html: There is also a nice basis for the filter atTop. The lemma Filter.HasBasis.tendsto_iff allows us to reformulate a statement of the form Tendsto f F G given bases for F and G. Putting these pieces together gives us essentially the notion of convergence that we used in Section 3.6.
zh: 滤子 atTop 也有一个很好的基。引理 Filter.HasBasis.tendsto_iff 允许我们在给定 F 和 G 的基之后，重新表述形如 Tendsto f F G 的陈述。把这些内容结合起来，基本上就得到我们在Section 3.6中使用的收敛概念。

text: We now show how filters facilitate working with properties that hold for sufficiently large numbers or for points that are sufficiently close to a given point. In Section 3.6 , we were often faced with the situation where we knew that some property P n holds for sufficiently large n and that some other property Q n holds for sufficiently large n . Using cases twice gave us N_P and N_Q satisfying ∀ n ≥ N_P, P n and ∀ n ≥ N_Q, Q n . Using set N := max N_P N_Q , we could eventually prove ∀ n ≥ N, P n ∧ Q n . Doing this repeatedly becomes tiresome.
html: We now show how filters facilitate working with properties that hold for sufficiently large numbers or for points that are sufficiently close to a given point. In Section 3.6, we were often faced with the situation where we knew that some property P n holds for sufficiently large n and that some other property Q n holds for sufficiently large n. Using cases twice gave us N_P and N_Q satisfying ∀ n ≥ N_P, P n and ∀ n ≥ N_Q, Q n. Using set N := max N_P N_Q, we could eventually prove ∀ n ≥ N, P n ∧ Q n. Doing this repeatedly becomes tiresome.
zh: 现在说明滤子如何帮助处理“对充分大的数成立”或“对充分接近某点的点成立”的性质。在Section 3.6中，我们经常遇到这样的情形：已知某性质 P n 对充分大的 n 成立，且另一个性质 Q n 也对充分大的 n 成立。使用两次 cases 会得到 N_P 与 N_Q，满足 ∀ n ≥ N_P, P n 和 ∀ n ≥ N_Q, Q n。再使用 set N := max N_P N_Q，最终可以证明 ∀ n ≥ N, P n ∧ Q n。反复这样做会变得繁琐。

text: We can do better by noting that the statement “ P n and Q n hold for large enough n ” means that we have {n | P n} ∈ atTop and {n | Q n} ∈ atTop . The fact that atTop is a filter implies that the intersection of two elements of atTop is again in atTop , so we have {n | P n ∧ Q n} ∈ atTop . Writing {n | P n} ∈ atTop is unpleasant, but we can use the more suggestive notation ∀ᶠ n in atTop, P n . Here the superscripted f stands for “Filter.” You can think of the notation as saying that for all n in the “set of very large numbers,” P n holds. The ∀ᶠ notation stands for Filter.Eventually , and the lemma Filter.Eventually.and uses the intersection property of filters to do what we just described:

The fact that atTop is a filter implies that the intersection of two elements of atTop is again in atTop, so we have {n | P n ∧ Q n} ∈ atTop. Writing {n | P n} ∈ atTop is unpleasant, but we can use the more suggestive notation ∀ᶠ n in atTop, P n. The ∀ᶠ notation stands for Filter.Eventually, and the lemma Filter.Eventually.and uses the intersection property of filters to do what we just described:
zh: 可以做得更好：注意陈述“P n 与 Q n 对充分大的 n 成立”意味着有 {n | P n} ∈ atTop 和 {n | Q n} ∈ atTop。由于 atTop 是滤子，atTop 的两个元素之交仍在 atTop 中，因此有 {n | P n ∧ Q n} ∈ atTop。写 {n | P n} ∈ atTop 并不方便，但可以使用更具提示性的记号 ∀ᶠ n in atTop, P n。这里上标 f 代表“Filter”。可以把这个记号理解为：对“非常大的数的集合”中的所有 n，P n 成立。∀ᶠ 记号代表 Filter.Eventually，而引理 Filter.Eventually.and 使用滤子的交性质完成刚才描述的事情：

text: This notation is so convenient and intuitive that we also have specializations when P is an equality or inequality statement. For example, let u and v be two sequences of real numbers, and let us show that if u n and v n coincide for sufficiently large n then u tends to x₀ if and only if v tends to x₀ . First we’ll use the generic Eventually and then the one specialized for the equality predicate, EventuallyEq . The two statements are definitionally equivalent so the same proof work in both cases.
html: This notation is so convenient and intuitive that we also have specializations when P is an equality or inequality statement. For example, let u and v be two sequences of real numbers, and let us show that if u n and v n coincide for sufficiently large n then u tends to x₀ if and only if v tends to x₀. First we’ll use the generic Eventually and then the one specialized for the equality predicate, EventuallyEq. The two statements are definitionally equivalent so the same proof work in both cases.
zh: 这个记号非常方便且直观，因此当 P 是等式或不等式陈述时也有专门版本。例如，令 u 和 v 为两个实数序列，证明若 u n 与 v n 对充分大的 n 相等，则 u 趋向 x₀ 当且仅当 v 趋向 x₀。我们先使用一般的 Eventually，再使用针对等式谓词的专门版本 EventuallyEq。这两个陈述按定义等价，因此同一个证明在两种情形中都适用。
