text: Readers who know about measure theory will note that the filter μ.ae of sets whose complement has measure zero (aka “the set consisting of almost every point”) is not very useful as the source or target of Tendsto , but it can be conveniently used with Eventually to say that a property holds for almost every point.
html: Readers who know about measure theory will note that the filter μ.ae of sets whose complement has measure zero (aka “the set consisting of almost every point”) is not very useful as the source or target of Tendsto, but it can be conveniently used with Eventually to say that a property holds for almost every point.
zh: 了解测度论的读者会注意到，由补集测度为零的集合组成的滤子 μ.ae（也就是“几乎每一点组成的集合”）作为 Tendsto 的源或目标并不十分有用，但它可以很方便地与 Eventually 一起使用，用来表达某个性质几乎处处成立。

text: There is a dual version of ∀ᶠ x in F, P x , which is occasionally useful: ∃ᶠ x in F, P x means {x | ¬P x} ∉ F . For example, ∃ᶠ n in atTop, P n means there are arbitrarily large n such that P n holds. The ∃ᶠ notation stands for Filter.Frequently .
html: There is a dual version of ∀ᶠ x in F, P x, which is occasionally useful: ∃ᶠ x in F, P x means {x | ¬P x} ∉ F. For example, ∃ᶠ n in atTop, P n means there are arbitrarily large n such that P n holds. The ∃ᶠ notation stands for Filter.Frequently.
zh: ∀ᶠ x in F, P x 有一个偶尔很有用的对偶版本：∃ᶠ x in F, P x 意味着 {x | ¬P x} ∉ F。例如，∃ᶠ n in atTop, P n 表示存在任意大的 n 使得 P n 成立。记号 ∃ᶠ 代表 Filter.Frequently。

text: For a more sophisticated example, consider the following statement about a sequence u , a set M , and a value x :
html: For a more sophisticated example, consider the following statement about a sequence u, a set M, and a value x:
zh: 作为一个更复杂的例子，考虑下面关于序列 u、集合 M 和值 x 的陈述：

text: If u converges to x and u n belongs to M for sufficiently large n then x is in the closure of M .
html: If u converges to x and u n belongs to M for sufficiently large n then x is in the closure of M.
zh: 若 u 收敛到 x，并且对充分大的 n，u n 属于 M，则 x 属于 M 的闭包。

text: This can be formalized as follows:
html: This can be formalized as follows:
zh: 这可以形式化如下：

text: Tendsto u atTop (𝓝 x) → (∀ᶠ n in atTop, u n ∈ M) → x ∈ closure M .
html: Tendsto u atTop (𝓝 x) → (∀ᶠ n in atTop, u n ∈ M) → x ∈ closure M.
zh: 即 Tendsto u atTop (𝓝 x) → (∀ᶠ n in atTop, u n ∈ M) → x ∈ closure M。

text: This is a special case of the theorem mem_closure_of_tendsto from the topology library. See if you can prove it using the quoted lemmas, using the fact that ClusterPt x F means (𝓝 x ⊓ F).NeBot and that, by definition, the assumption ∀ᶠ n in atTop, u n ∈ M means M ∈ map u atTop .
html: This is a special case of the theorem mem_closure_of_tendsto from the topology library. See if you can prove it using the quoted lemmas, using the fact that ClusterPt x F means (𝓝 x ⊓ F).NeBot and that, by definition, the assumption ∀ᶠ n in atTop, u n ∈ M means M ∈ map u atTop.
zh: 这是拓扑库中定理 mem_closure_of_tendsto 的一个特例。请尝试使用引用的引理证明它；其中要用到 ClusterPt x F 表示 (𝓝 x ⊓ F).NeBot，并且按定义，假设 ∀ᶠ n in atTop, u n ∈ M 表示 M ∈ map u atTop。

text: Examples in the previous section focus on sequences of real numbers. In this section we will go up a bit in generality and focus on metric spaces. A metric space is a type X equipped with a distance function dist : X → X → ℝ which is a generalization of the function fun x y ↦ |x - y| from the case where X = ℝ .
html: Examples in the previous section focus on sequences of real numbers. In this section we will go up a bit in generality and focus on metric spaces. A metric space is a type X equipped with a distance function dist : X → X → ℝ which is a generalization of the function fun x y ↦ |x - y| from the case where X = ℝ.
zh: 上一节的例子集中于实数序列。本节将稍微提高一般性，转向度量空间。度量空间是一个类型 X，配备距离函数 dist : X → X → ℝ；它推广了 X = ℝ 情形中的函数 fun x y ↦ |x - y|。

text: Introducing such a space is easy and we will check all properties required from the distance function.
html: Introducing such a space is easy and we will check all properties required from the distance function.
zh: 引入这样的空间很容易，并且我们将检查距离函数所需的所有性质。

text: Note we also have variants where the distance can be infinite or where dist a b can be zero without having a = b or both. They are called EMetricSpace , PseudoMetricSpace and PseudoEMetricSpace respectively (here “e” stands for “extended”).
html: Note we also have variants where the distance can be infinite or where dist a b can be zero without having a = b or both. They are called EMetricSpace, PseudoMetricSpace and PseudoEMetricSpace respectively (here “e” stands for “extended”).
zh: 注意，还有一些变体允许距离为无穷，或者允许 dist a b 为零而不必有 a = b，或同时允许这两种情况。它们分别称为 EMetricSpace、PseudoMetricSpace 和 PseudoEMetricSpace（这里的“e”代表“extended”）。

text: Note that our journey from ℝ to metric spaces jumped over the special case of normed spaces that also require linear algebra and will be explained as part of the calculus chapter.
html: Note that our journey from ℝ to metric spaces jumped over the special case of normed spaces that also require linear algebra and will be explained as part of the calculus chapter.
zh: 注意，从 ℝ 到度量空间的路径跳过了赋范空间这一特殊情形；赋范空间还需要线性代数，并将在微积分章节中解释。

text: Using distance functions, we can already define convergent sequences and continuous functions between metric spaces. They are actually defined in a more general setting covered in the next section, but we have lemmas recasting the definition in terms of distances.
html: Using distance functions, we can already define convergent sequences and continuous functions between metric spaces. They are actually defined in a more general setting covered in the next section, but we have lemmas recasting the definition in terms of distances.
zh: 使用距离函数，已经可以定义收敛序列以及度量空间之间的连续函数。实际上，它们是在下一节涉及的更一般设置中定义的，但我们有用距离重新表述定义的引理。

text: A lot of lemmas have some continuity assumptions, so we end up proving a lot of continuity results and there is a continuity tactic devoted to this task. Let’s prove a continuity statement that will be needed in an exercise below. Notice that Lean knows how to treat a product of two metric spaces as a metric space; it chooses the sup norm, so dist (x₁, y₁) (x₂, y₂) = max (dist x₁ x₂) (dist y₁ y₂) holds by reflexivity. As a result, it makes sense to consider continuous functions from X × X to ℝ . In particular the (uncurried version of the) distance function is such a function.
html: A lot of lemmas have some continuity assumptions, so we end up proving a lot of continuity results and there is a continuity tactic devoted to this task. Let’s prove a continuity statement that will be needed in an exercise below. Notice that Lean knows how to treat a product of two metric spaces as a metric space; it chooses the sup norm, so dist (x₁, y₁) (x₂, y₂) = max (dist x₁ x₂) (dist y₁ y₂) holds by reflexivity. As a result, it makes sense to consider continuous functions from X × X to ℝ. In particular the (uncurried version of the) distance function is such a function.
zh: 许多引理带有连续性假设，因此我们最终会证明大量连续性结果，并且有一个专门完成这项任务的 continuity 策略。先证明下面练习中将要用到的一个连续性陈述。注意，Lean 知道如何把两个度量空间的积视为度量空间；它选择上确界范数，因此 dist (x₁, y₁) (x₂, y₂) = max (dist x₁ x₂) (dist y₁ y₂) 由自反性成立。因此，考虑从 X × X 到 ℝ 的连续函数是有意义的。特别地，距离函数的非柯里化版本就是这样的函数。

text: This tactic is a bit slow, so it is also useful to know how to do it by hand. We first need to use that fun p : X × X ↦ f p.1 is continuous because it is the composition of f , which is continuous by assumption hf , and the projection prod.fst whose continuity is the content of the lemma continuous_fst . The composition property is Continuous.comp which is in the Continuous namespace so we can use dot notation to compress Continuous.comp hf continuous_fst into hf.comp continuous_fst which is actually more readable since it really reads as composing our assumption and our lemma. We can do the same for the second component to get continuity of fun p : X × X ↦ f p.2 . We then assemble those two continuities using Continuous.prod_mk to get (hf.comp continuous_fst).prod_mk (hf.comp continuous_snd) : Continuous (fun p : X × X ↦ (f p.1, f p.2)) and compose once more to get our full proof.
html: This tactic is a bit slow, so it is also useful to know how to do it by hand. We first need to use that fun p : X × X ↦ f p.1 is continuous because it is the composition of f, which is continuous by assumption hf, and the projection prod.fst whose continuity is the content of the lemma continuous_fst. The composition property is Continuous.comp which is in the Continuous namespace so we can use dot notation to compress Continuous.comp hf continuous_fst into hf.comp continuous_fst which is actually more readable since it really reads as composing our assumption and our lemma. We can do the same for the second component to get continuity of fun p : X × X ↦ f p.2. We then assemble those two continuities using Continuous.prod_mk to get (hf.comp continuous_fst).prod_mk (hf.comp continuous_snd) : Continuous (fun p : X × X ↦ (f p.1, f p.2)) and compose once more to get our full proof.
zh: 这个策略有些慢，因此知道如何手动完成也很有用。首先需要使用：函数 fun p : X × X ↦ f p.1 是连续的，因为它是 f 与投影 prod.fst 的复合；其中 f 根据假设 hf 连续，而 prod.fst 的连续性正是引理 continuous_fst 的内容。复合性质是 Continuous.comp，它位于 Continuous 命名空间中，因此可以使用点记号把 Continuous.comp hf continuous_fst 压缩为 hf.comp continuous_fst；后者实际上更可读，因为它确实读作把我们的假设与引理复合。对第二个分量也可以类似处理，得到 fun p : X × X ↦ f p.2 的连续性。然后用 Continuous.prod_mk 组装这两个连续性，得到 (hf.comp continuous_fst).prod_mk (hf.comp continuous_snd) : Continuous (fun p : X × X ↦ (f p.1, f p.2))，再复合一次即可得到完整证明。

text: The combination of Continuous.prod_mk and continuous_dist via Continuous.comp feels clunky, even when heavily using dot notation as above. A more serious issue is that this nice proof requires a lot of planning. Lean accepts the above proof term because it is a full term proving a statement which is definitionally equivalent to our goal, the crucial definition to unfold being that of a composition of functions. Indeed our target function fun p : X × X ↦ dist (f p.1) (f p.2) is not presented as a composition. The proof term we provided proves continuity of dist ∘ (fun p : X × X ↦ (f p.1, f p.2)) which happens to be definitionally equal to our target function. But if we try to build this proof gradually using tactics starting with apply continuous_dist.comp then Lean’s elaborator will fail to recognize a composition and refuse to apply this lemma. It is especially bad at this when products of types are involved.
html: The combination of Continuous.prod_mk and continuous_dist via Continuous.comp feels clunky, even when heavily using dot notation as above. A more serious issue is that this nice proof requires a lot of planning. Lean accepts the above proof term because it is a full term proving a statement which is definitionally equivalent to our goal, the crucial definition to unfold being that of a composition of functions. Indeed our target function fun p : X × X ↦ dist (f p.1) (f p.2) is not presented as a composition. The proof term we provided proves continuity of dist ∘ (fun p : X × X ↦ (f p.1, f p.2)) which happens to be definitionally equal to our target function. But if we try to build this proof gradually using tactics starting with apply continuous_dist.comp then Lean’s elaborator will fail to recognize a composition and refuse to apply this lemma. It is especially bad at this when products of types are involved.
zh: 即使像上面那样大量使用点记号，通过 Continuous.comp 把 Continuous.prod_mk 与 continuous_dist 组合起来仍显得笨拙。更严重的问题是，这个漂亮的证明需要相当多预先规划。Lean 接受上面的证明项，是因为它是一个完整项，证明了一个与目标按定义等价的陈述；关键是展开函数复合的定义。事实上，我们的目标函数 fun p : X × X ↦ dist (f p.1) (f p.2) 并不是以复合形式呈现的。我们给出的证明项证明的是 dist ∘ (fun p : X × X ↦ (f p.1, f p.2)) 的连续性，而它恰好与目标函数按定义相等。但如果尝试用策略逐步构造这个证明，先写 apply continuous_dist.comp，Lean 的 elaborator 将无法识别出复合，从而拒绝应用该引理。当涉及类型的积时，这种情况尤其明显。

text: A better lemma to apply here is Continuous.dist {f g : X → Y} : Continuous f → Continuous g → Continuous (fun x ↦ dist (f x) (g x)) which is nicer to Lean’s elaborator and also provides a shorter proof when directly providing a full proof term, as can be seen from the following two new proofs of the above statement:
html: A better lemma to apply here is Continuous.dist {f g : X → Y} : Continuous f → Continuous g → Continuous (fun x ↦ dist (f x) (g x)) which is nicer to Lean’s elaborator and also provides a shorter proof when directly providing a full proof term, as can be seen from the following two new proofs of the above statement:
zh: 这里更适合应用的引理是 Continuous.dist {f g : X → Y} : Continuous f → Continuous g → Continuous (fun x ↦ dist (f x) (g x))；它对 Lean 的 elaborator 更友好，并且在直接给出完整证明项时也能产生更短的证明，下面两个对上述陈述的新证明展示了这一点：

text: Note that, without the elaboration issue coming from composition, another way to compress our proof would be to use Continuous.prod_map which is sometimes useful and gives
html: Note that, without the elaboration issue coming from composition, another way to compress our proof would be to use Continuous.prod_map which is sometimes useful and gives
zh: 注意，如果没有来自复合的 elaboration 问题，压缩证明的另一种方式是使用 Continuous.prod_map；它有时很有用，并给出

text: Since it is sad to decide between a version which is better for elaboration and a version which is shorter to type, let us wrap this discussion with a last bit of compression offered by Continuous.fst' which allows to compress hf.comp continuous_fst to hf.fst' (and the same with snd ) and get our final proof, now bordering obfuscation.
html: Since it is sad to decide between a version which is better for elaboration and a version which is shorter to type, let us wrap this discussion with a last bit of compression offered by Continuous.fst' which allows to compress hf.comp continuous_fst to hf.fst' (and the same with snd) and get our final proof, now bordering obfuscation.
zh: 既要在更利于 elaboration 的版本和更短的输入版本之间取舍，多少令人遗憾；因此我们用 Continuous.fst' 提供的最后一点压缩来结束这段讨论。它允许把 hf.comp continuous_fst 压缩为 hf.fst'（对 snd 也类似），从而得到最后一个证明；此时已经接近晦涩。

text: It’s your turn now to prove some continuity lemma. After trying the continuity tactic, you will need Continuous.add , continuous_pow and continuous_id to do it by hand.
html: It’s your turn now to prove some continuity lemma. After trying the continuity tactic, you will need Continuous.add, continuous_pow and continuous_id to do it by hand.
zh: 现在轮到你证明一些连续性引理。尝试 continuity 策略之后，若要手动完成，将需要 Continuous.add、continuous_pow 和 continuous_id。

text: So far we saw continuity as a global notion, but one can also define continuity at a point.
html: So far we saw continuity as a global notion, but one can also define continuity at a point.
zh: 到目前为止，我们把连续性视为全局概念；但也可以定义一点处连续。

text: Once we have a distance function, the most important geometric definitions are (open) balls and closed balls.
html: Once we have a distance function, the most important geometric definitions are (open) balls and closed balls.
zh: 一旦有了距离函数，最重要的几何定义就是（开）球和闭球。

text: Note that r is any real number here, there is no sign restriction. Of course some statements do require a radius condition.
html: Note that r is any real number here, there is no sign restriction. Of course some statements do require a radius condition.
zh: 注意，这里的 <cite>r</cite> 是任意实数，没有符号限制。当然，某些陈述确实需要半径条件。

text: Once we have balls, we can define open sets. They are actually defined in a more general setting covered in the next section, but we have lemmas recasting the definition in terms of balls.
html: Once we have balls, we can define open sets. They are actually defined in a more general setting covered in the next section, but we have lemmas recasting the definition in terms of balls.
zh: 有了球之后，就可以定义开集。开集实际上是在下一节涉及的更一般设置中定义的，但我们有用球重新表述该定义的引理。

text: Then closed sets are sets whose complement is open. Their important property is they are closed under limits. The closure of a set is the smallest closed set containing it.
html: Then closed sets are sets whose complement is open. Their important property is they are closed under limits. The closure of a set is the smallest closed set containing it.
zh: 于是，闭集就是补集为开集的集合。它们的重要性质是对极限封闭。一个集合的闭包是包含它的最小闭集。

text: Do the next exercise without using mem_closure_iff_seq_limit
html: Do the next exercise without using mem_closure_iff_seq_limit
zh: 完成下一个练习时不要使用 <cite>mem_closure_iff_seq_limit</cite>。

text: Remember from the filters sections that neighborhood filters play a big role in Mathlib. In the metric space context, the crucial point is that balls provide bases for those filters. The main lemmas here are Metric.nhds_basis_ball and Metric.nhds_basis_closedBall that claim this for open and closed balls with positive radius. The center point is an implicit argument so we can invoke Filter.HasBasis.mem_iff as in the following example.
html: Remember from the filters sections that neighborhood filters play a big role in Mathlib. In the metric space context, the crucial point is that balls provide bases for those filters. The main lemmas here are Metric.nhds_basis_ball and Metric.nhds_basis_closedBall that claim this for open and closed balls with positive radius. The center point is an implicit argument so we can invoke Filter.HasBasis.mem_iff as in the following example.
zh: 请回忆滤子部分中邻域滤子在 Mathlib 中的重要作用。在度量空间语境下，关键点是球为这些滤子提供了基。这里的主要引理是 Metric.nhds_basis_ball 和 Metric.nhds_basis_closedBall，它们分别对正半径开球和闭球断言这一点。中心点是隐式参数，因此可以像下面的例子一样调用 Filter.HasBasis.mem_iff。

text: Compactness is an important topological notion. It distinguishes subsets of a metric space that enjoy the same kind of properties as segments in the reals compared to other intervals:
html: Compactness is an important topological notion. It distinguishes subsets of a metric space that enjoy the same kind of properties as segments in the reals compared to other intervals:
zh: 紧性是重要的拓扑概念。它把度量空间中具有与实数线段相同性质的子集同其他区间区分开来：

text: Let us first check that the unit interval in the reals is indeed a compact set, and then check the above claims for compact sets in general metric spaces. In the second statement we only need continuity on the given set so we will use ContinuousOn instead of Continuous , and we will give separate statements for the minimum and the maximum. Of course all these results are deduced from more general versions, some of which will be discussed in later sections.
html: Let us first check that the unit interval in the reals is indeed a compact set, and then check the above claims for compact sets in general metric spaces. In the second statement we only need continuity on the given set so we will use ContinuousOn instead of Continuous, and we will give separate statements for the minimum and the maximum. Of course all these results are deduced from more general versions, some of which will be discussed in later sections.
zh: 先检查实数中的单位区间确实是紧集，然后在一般度量空间中检查上面的紧集性质。第二个陈述只需要在给定集合上的连续性，所以将使用 ContinuousOn 而不是 Continuous，并且会分别给出最小值和最大值的陈述。当然，这些结果都由更一般的版本推出，其中一些会在后面的章节中讨论。

text: We can also specify that a metric spaces is globally compact, using an extra Prop -valued type class:
html: We can also specify that a metric spaces is globally compact, using an extra Prop-valued type class:
zh: 也可以用一个额外的取值于 Prop 的类型类，指定一个度量空间整体上是紧的：

text: In a compact metric space any closed set is compact, this is IsClosed.isCompact .
html: In a compact metric space any closed set is compact, this is IsClosed.isCompact.
zh: 在紧度量空间中，任意闭集都是紧的；这就是 IsClosed.isCompact。

text: We now turn to uniformity notions on metric spaces : uniformly continuous functions, Cauchy sequences and completeness. Again those are defined in a more general context but we have lemmas in the metric name space to access their elementary definitions. We start with uniform continuity.
html: We now turn to uniformity notions on metric spaces : uniformly continuous functions, Cauchy sequences and completeness. Again those are defined in a more general context but we have lemmas in the metric name space to access their elementary definitions. We start with uniform continuity.
zh: 现在转向度量空间上的一致性概念：一致连续函数、柯西序列和完备性。它们同样是在更一般的语境中定义的，但我们有度量命名空间中的引理可用来访问其初等定义。先从一致连续性开始。

text: In order to practice manipulating all those definitions, we will prove that continuous functions from a compact metric space to a metric space are uniformly continuous (we will see a more general version in a later section).
html: In order to practice manipulating all those definitions, we will prove that continuous functions from a compact metric space to a metric space are uniformly continuous (we will see a more general version in a later section).
zh: 为了练习操作这些定义，我们将证明从紧度量空间到度量空间的连续函数是一致连续的（稍后会看到一个更一般的版本）。

text: We will first give an informal sketch. Let f : X → Y be a continuous function from a compact metric space to a metric space. We fix ε > 0 and start looking for some δ .
html: We will first give an informal sketch. Let f : X → Y be a continuous function from a compact metric space to a metric space. We fix ε > 0 and start looking for some δ.
zh: 先给出一个非形式化证明梗概。设 f : X → Y 是从紧度量空间到度量空间的连续函数。固定 ε > 0，开始寻找某个 δ。

text: Let φ : X × X → ℝ := fun p ↦ dist (f p.1) (f p.2) and let K := { p : X × X | ε ≤ φ p } . Observe φ is continuous since f and distance are continuous. And K is clearly closed (use isClosed_le ) hence compact since X is compact and Lean knows that the product of compact spaces is compact.
html: Let φ : X × X → ℝ := fun p ↦ dist (f p.1) (f p.2) and let K := { p : X × X | ε ≤ φ p }. Observe φ is continuous since f and distance are continuous. And K is clearly closed (use isClosed_le) hence compact since X is compact and Lean knows that the product of compact spaces is compact.
zh: 令 φ : X × X → ℝ := fun p ↦ dist (f p.1) (f p.2)，并令 K := { p : X × X | ε ≤ φ p }。注意 φ 连续，因为 f 和距离函数都连续。并且 K 显然是闭的（使用 isClosed_le），因此它是紧的，因为 X 紧且 Lean 知道紧空间的积仍为紧。

text: Then we discuss two possibilities using eq_empty_or_nonempty . If K is empty then we are clearly done (we can set δ = 1 for instance). So let’s assume K is not empty, and use the extreme value theorem to choose (x₀, x₁) attaining the infimum of the distance function on K . We can then set δ = dist x₀ x₁ and check everything works.
html: Then we discuss two possibilities using eq_empty_or_nonempty. If K is empty then we are clearly done (we can set δ = 1 for instance). So let’s assume K is not empty, and use the extreme value theorem to choose (x₀, x₁) attaining the infimum of the distance function on K. We can then set δ = dist x₀ x₁ and check everything works.
zh: 然后用 eq_empty_or_nonempty 分情况讨论。若 K 为空，则显然完成证明（例如可以取 δ = 1）。因此假设 K 非空，并使用极值定理选择 (x₀, x₁)，使距离函数在 K 上取得下确界。随后可以取 δ = dist x₀ x₁，并检查一切成立。

text: A Cauchy sequence in a metric space is a sequence whose terms get closer and closer to each other. There are a couple of equivalent ways to state that idea. In particular converging sequences are Cauchy. The converse is true only in so-called complete spaces.
html: A Cauchy sequence in a metric space is a sequence whose terms get closer and closer to each other. There are a couple of equivalent ways to state that idea. In particular converging sequences are Cauchy. The converse is true only in so-called complete spaces.
zh: 度量空间中的柯西序列，是各项彼此越来越接近的序列。表达这一思想有若干等价方式。特别地，收敛序列是柯西序列。反命题只在所谓完备空间中成立。

text: We’ll practice using this definition by proving a convenient criterion which is a special case of a criterion appearing in Mathlib. This is also a good opportunity to practice using big sums in a geometric context. In addition to the explanations from the filters section, you will probably need tendsto_pow_atTop_nhds_zero_of_lt_one , Tendsto.mul and dist_le_range_sum_dist .
html: We’ll practice using this definition by proving a convenient criterion which is a special case of a criterion appearing in Mathlib. This is also a good opportunity to practice using big sums in a geometric context. In addition to the explanations from the filters section, you will probably need tendsto_pow_atTop_nhds_zero_of_lt_one, Tendsto.mul and dist_le_range_sum_dist.
zh: 我们将通过证明一个方便的判别准则来练习使用这个定义；它是 Mathlib 中某个判别准则的特例。这也是在几何语境中练习使用大和式的好机会。除了滤子部分的说明之外，你可能还需要 tendsto_pow_atTop_nhds_zero_of_lt_one、Tendsto.mul 和 dist_le_range_sum_dist。

text: We are ready for the final boss of this section: Baire’s theorem for complete metric spaces! The proof skeleton below shows interesting techniques. It uses the choose tactic in its exclamation mark variant (you should experiment with removing this exclamation mark) and it shows how to define something inductively in the middle of a proof using Nat.rec_on .
html: We are ready for the final boss of this section: Baire’s theorem for complete metric spaces! The proof skeleton below shows interesting techniques. It uses the choose tactic in its exclamation mark variant (you should experiment with removing this exclamation mark) and it shows how to define something inductively in the middle of a proof using Nat.rec_on.
zh: 现在准备迎接本节最后的难题：完备度量空间的贝尔定理！下面的证明骨架展示了一些有趣技巧。它使用感叹号版本的 choose 策略（你应当尝试去掉这个感叹号看看会发生什么），并展示如何在证明中间使用 Nat.rec_on 归纳地定义某个对象。

text: We now go up in generality and introduce topological spaces. We will review the two main ways to define topological spaces and then explain how the category of topological spaces is much better behaved than the category of metric spaces. Note that we won’t be using Mathlib category theory here, only having a somewhat categorical point of view.
html: We now go up in generality and introduce topological spaces. We will review the two main ways to define topological spaces and then explain how the category of topological spaces is much better behaved than the category of metric spaces. Note that we won’t be using Mathlib category theory here, only having a somewhat categorical point of view.
zh: 现在进一步提高一般性，引入拓扑空间。我们将回顾定义拓扑空间的两种主要方式，然后解释为什么拓扑空间范畴比度量空间范畴表现得好得多。注意，这里不会使用 Mathlib 的范畴论，只是采取一种略带范畴意味的观点。

text: The first way to think about the transition from metric spaces to topological spaces is that we only remember the notion of open sets (or equivalently the notion of closed sets). From this point of view, a topological space is a type equipped with a collection of sets that are called open sets. This collection has to satisfy a number of axioms presented below (this collection is slightly redundant but we will ignore that).
html: The first way to think about the transition from metric spaces to topological spaces is that we only remember the notion of open sets (or equivalently the notion of closed sets). From this point of view, a topological space is a type equipped with a collection of sets that are called open sets. This collection has to satisfy a number of axioms presented below (this collection is slightly redundant but we will ignore that).
zh: 从度量空间过渡到拓扑空间的第一种理解方式是：只保留开集概念（或等价地，闭集概念）。从这个观点看，拓扑空间是一个类型，配备一族称为开集的集合。这个集合族必须满足下面给出的若干公理（这份数据略有冗余，但我们将忽略这一点）。

text: Closed sets are then defined as sets whose complement is open. A function between topological spaces is (globally) continuous if all preimages of open sets are open.
html: Closed sets are then defined as sets whose complement is open. A function between topological spaces is (globally) continuous if all preimages of open sets are open.
zh: 闭集于是定义为补集为开集的集合。拓扑空间之间的函数称为（全局）连续，当且仅当所有开集的原像都是开集。

text: With this definition we already see that, compared to metric spaces, topological spaces only remember enough information to talk about continuous functions: two topological structures on a type are the same if and only if they have the same continuous functions (indeed the identity function will be continuous in both direction if and only if the two structures have the same open sets).
html: With this definition we already see that, compared to metric spaces, topological spaces only remember enough information to talk about continuous functions: two topological structures on a type are the same if and only if they have the same continuous functions (indeed the identity function will be continuous in both direction if and only if the two structures have the same open sets).
zh: 用这个定义已经可以看到，与度量空间相比，拓扑空间只保留了足以讨论连续函数的信息：一个类型上的两个拓扑结构相同，当且仅当它们具有相同的连续函数（事实上，恒等函数在两个方向都连续，当且仅当这两个结构具有相同的开集）。

text: However as soon as we move on to continuity at a point we see the limitations of the approach based on open sets. In Mathlib we frequently think of topological spaces as types equipped with a neighborhood filter 𝓝 x attached to each point x (the corresponding function X → Filter X satisfies certain conditions explained further down). Remember from the filters section that these gadgets play two related roles. First 𝓝 x is seen as the generalized set of points of X that are close to x . And then it is seen as giving a way to say, for any predicate P : X → Prop , that this predicate holds for points that are close enough to x . Let us state that f : X → Y is continuous at x . The purely filtery way is to say that the direct image under f of the generalized set of points that are close to x is contained in the generalized set of points that are close to f x . Recall this is spelled either map f (𝓝 x) ≤ 𝓝 (f x) or Tendsto f (𝓝 x) (𝓝 (f x)) .
html: However as soon as we move on to continuity at a point we see the limitations of the approach based on open sets. In Mathlib we frequently think of topological spaces as types equipped with a neighborhood filter 𝓝 x attached to each point x (the corresponding function X → Filter X satisfies certain conditions explained further down). Remember from the filters section that these gadgets play two related roles. First 𝓝 x is seen as the generalized set of points of X that are close to x. And then it is seen as giving a way to say, for any predicate P : X → Prop, that this predicate holds for points that are close enough to x. Let us state that f : X → Y is continuous at x. The purely filtery way is to say that the direct image under f of the generalized set of points that are close to x is contained in the generalized set of points that are close to f x. Recall this is spelled either map f (𝓝 x) ≤ 𝓝 (f x) or Tendsto f (𝓝 x) (𝓝 (f x)).
zh: 然而，一旦转向一点处连续，就能看到基于开集的方法的局限。在 Mathlib 中，我们经常把拓扑空间理解为这样的类型：每个点 x 都配有一个邻域滤子 𝓝 x（相应函数 X → Filter X 满足后面解释的若干条件）。请回忆滤子部分中这些对象的两个相关作用。首先，𝓝 x 被看作 X 中接近 x 的点组成的广义集合。其次，它给出一种方式，用于表达对任意谓词 P : X → Prop，该谓词在足够接近 x 的点处成立。现在陈述 f : X → Y 在 x 处连续。完全滤子化的表述是：f 下接近 x 的点组成的广义集合的直接像，包含于接近 f x 的点组成的广义集合。回忆这可以写作 map f (𝓝 x) ≤ 𝓝 (f x) 或 Tendsto f (𝓝 x) (𝓝 (f x))。

text: One can also spell it using both neighborhoods seen as ordinary sets and a neighborhood filter seen as a generalized set: “for any neighborhood U of f x , all points close to x are sent to U ”. Note that the proof is again Iff.rfl , this point of view is definitionally equivalent to the previous one.
html: One can also spell it using both neighborhoods seen as ordinary sets and a neighborhood filter seen as a generalized set: “for any neighborhood U of f x, all points close to x are sent to U”. Note that the proof is again Iff.rfl, this point of view is definitionally equivalent to the previous one.
zh: 也可以同时把邻域看作普通集合，而把邻域滤子看作广义集合来表述：“对 f x 的任意邻域 U，所有足够接近 x 的点都会被送入 U”。注意，证明再次是 Iff.rfl；这个观点与前一个观点按定义等价。

text: We now explain how to go from one point of view to the other. In terms of open sets, we can simply define members of 𝓝 x as sets that contain an open set containing x .
html: We now explain how to go from one point of view to the other. In terms of open sets, we can simply define members of 𝓝 x as sets that contain an open set containing x.
zh: 现在解释如何在两种观点之间转换。用开集来表述时，可以简单地把 𝓝 x 的成员定义为包含某个含有 x 的开集的集合。

text: To go in the other direction we need to discuss the condition that 𝓝 : X → Filter X must satisfy in order to be the neighborhood function of a topology.
html: To go in the other direction we need to discuss the condition that 𝓝 : X → Filter X must satisfy in order to be the neighborhood function of a topology.
zh: 反向转换时，需要讨论 𝓝 : X → Filter X 必须满足什么条件，才能成为某个拓扑的邻域函数。

text: The first constraint is that 𝓝 x , seen as a generalized set, contains the set {x} seen as the generalized set pure x (explaining this weird name would be too much of a digression, so we simply accept it for now). Another way to say it is that if a predicate holds for points close to x then it holds at x .
html: The first constraint is that 𝓝 x, seen as a generalized set, contains the set {x} seen as the generalized set pure x (explaining this weird name would be too much of a digression, so we simply accept it for now). Another way to say it is that if a predicate holds for points close to x then it holds at x.
zh: 第一个约束是：把 𝓝 x 看作广义集合时，它包含把单点集 {x} 看作广义集合得到的 pure x（解释这个奇怪名称会偏离太远，所以暂且接受它）。另一种说法是：如果某个谓词对接近 x 的点成立，那么它在 x 处成立。

text: Then a more subtle requirement is that, for any predicate P : X → Prop and any x , if P y holds for y close to x then for y close to x and z close to y , P z holds. More precisely we have:
html: Then a more subtle requirement is that, for any predicate P : X → Prop and any x, if P y holds for y close to x then for y close to x and z close to y, P z holds. More precisely we have:
zh: 然后有一个更微妙的要求：对任意谓词 P : X → Prop 和任意 x，若对接近 x 的 y 有 P y 成立，则对接近 x 的 y 且接近 y 的 z，有 P z 成立。更精确地说，有：

text: Those two results characterize the functions X → Filter X that are neighborhood functions for a topological space structure on X . There is a still a function TopologicalSpace.mkOfNhds : (X → Filter X) → TopologicalSpace X but it will give back its input as a neighborhood function only if it satisfies the above two constraints. More precisely we have a lemma TopologicalSpace.nhds_mkOfNhds saying that in a different way and our next exercise deduces this different way from how we stated it above.
html: Those two results characterize the functions X → Filter X that are neighborhood functions for a topological space structure on X. There is a still a function TopologicalSpace.mkOfNhds : (X → Filter X) → TopologicalSpace X but it will give back its input as a neighborhood function only if it satisfies the above two constraints. More precisely we have a lemma TopologicalSpace.nhds_mkOfNhds saying that in a different way and our next exercise deduces this different way from how we stated it above.
zh: 这两个结果刻画了哪些函数 X → Filter X 是 X 上某个拓扑空间结构的邻域函数。仍然有一个函数 TopologicalSpace.mkOfNhds : (X → Filter X) → TopologicalSpace X；但只有当输入满足上述两个约束时，它才会把输入作为邻域函数返回。更精确地说，有一个引理 TopologicalSpace.nhds_mkOfNhds，它以另一种方式说明这一点；下一个练习将从我们上面的表述推出这种另一种表述。

text: Note that TopologicalSpace.mkOfNhds is not so frequently used, but it still good to know in what precise sense the neighborhood filters is all there is in a topological space structure.
html: Note that TopologicalSpace.mkOfNhds is not so frequently used, but it still good to know in what precise sense the neighborhood filters is all there is in a topological space structure.
zh: 注意，TopologicalSpace.mkOfNhds 并不经常使用；但仍然值得知道，在何种精确意义上，邻域滤子就是拓扑空间结构的全部内容。

text: The next thing to know in order to efficiently use topological spaces in Mathlib is that we use a lot of formal properties of TopologicalSpace : Type u → Type u . From a purely mathematical point of view, those formal properties are a very clean way to explain how topological spaces solve issues that metric spaces have. From this point of view, the issues solved by topological spaces is that metric spaces enjoy very little functoriality, and have very bad categorical properties in general. This comes on top of the fact already discussed that metric spaces contain a lot of geometrical information that is not topologically relevant.
html: The next thing to know in order to efficiently use topological spaces in Mathlib is that we use a lot of formal properties of TopologicalSpace : Type u → Type u. From a purely mathematical point of view, those formal properties are a very clean way to explain how topological spaces solve issues that metric spaces have. From this point of view, the issues solved by topological spaces is that metric spaces enjoy very little functoriality, and have very bad categorical properties in general. This comes on top of the fact already discussed that metric spaces contain a lot of geometrical information that is not topologically relevant.
zh: 为了在 Mathlib 中高效使用拓扑空间，接下来需要知道的是：我们大量使用 TopologicalSpace : Type u → Type u 的形式性质。从纯数学观点看，这些形式性质非常清楚地说明了拓扑空间如何解决度量空间存在的问题。从这个观点看，拓扑空间解决的问题是：度量空间的函子性很弱，并且总体上范畴性质很差。除此之外，前面也已经讨论过，度量空间包含大量拓扑上无关的几何信息。

text: Let us focus on functoriality first. A metric space structure can be induced on a subset or, equivalently, it can be pulled back by an injective map. But that’s pretty much everything. They cannot be pulled back by general map or pushed forward, even by surjective maps.
html: Let us focus on functoriality first. A metric space structure can be induced on a subset or, equivalently, it can be pulled back by an injective map. But that’s pretty much everything. They cannot be pulled back by general map or pushed forward, even by surjective maps.
zh: 先关注函子性。可以在子集上诱导度量空间结构；等价地，可以沿单射拉回度量结构。但这基本上就是全部。度量结构不能沿一般映射拉回，也不能推出，即使沿满射也不行。

text: In particular there is no sensible distance to put on a quotient of a metric space or on an uncountable product of metric spaces. Consider for instance the type ℝ → ℝ , seen as a product of copies of ℝ indexed by ℝ . We would like to say that pointwise convergence of sequences of functions is a respectable notion of convergence. But there is no distance on ℝ → ℝ that gives this notion of convergence. Relatedly, there is no distance ensuring that a map f : X → (ℝ → ℝ) is continuous if and only if fun x ↦ f x t is continuous for every t : ℝ .
html: In particular there is no sensible distance to put on a quotient of a metric space or on an uncountable product of metric spaces. Consider for instance the type ℝ → ℝ, seen as a product of copies of ℝ indexed by ℝ. We would like to say that pointwise convergence of sequences of functions is a respectable notion of convergence. But there is no distance on ℝ → ℝ that gives this notion of convergence. Relatedly, there is no distance ensuring that a map f : X → (ℝ → ℝ) is continuous if and only if fun x ↦ f x t is continuous for every t : ℝ.
zh: 特别地，不存在一种合理的方式给度量空间的商或不可数个度量空间的积赋予距离。举例来说，考虑类型 ℝ → ℝ，把它看成由 ℝ 指标的若干份 ℝ 的积。我们希望能够说函数序列的逐点收敛是一种正当的收敛概念。但 ℝ → ℝ 上不存在给出这一收敛概念的距离。相关地，也不存在一种距离，使得映射 f : X → (ℝ → ℝ) 连续当且仅当对每个 t : ℝ，fun x ↦ f x t 连续。

text: We now review the data used to solve all those issues. First we can use any map f : X → Y to push or pull topologies from one side to the other. Those two operations form a Galois connection.
html: We now review the data used to solve all those issues. First we can use any map f : X → Y to push or pull topologies from one side to the other. Those two operations form a Galois connection.
zh: 现在回顾用于解决这些问题的数据。首先，可以用任意映射 f : X → Y 从一侧向另一侧推出或拉回拓扑。这两个操作构成伽罗瓦连接。

text: Those operations are compatible with composition of functions. As usual, pushing forward is covariant and pulling back is contravariant, see coinduced_compose and induced_compose . On paper we will use notations \(f_*T\) for TopologicalSpace.coinduced f T and \(f^*T\) for TopologicalSpace.induced f T .
html: Those operations are compatible with composition of functions. As usual, pushing forward is covariant and pulling back is contravariant, see coinduced_compose and induced_compose. On paper we will use notations \(f_*T\) for TopologicalSpace.coinduced f T and \(f^*T\) for TopologicalSpace.induced f T.
zh: 这些操作与函数复合相容。照常，推出是协变的，拉回是反变的；参见 coinduced_compose 与 induced_compose。纸笔记号中，我们将用 \(f_*T\) 表示 TopologicalSpace.coinduced f T，用 \(f^*T\) 表示 TopologicalSpace.induced f T。

text: Then the next big piece is a complete lattice structure on TopologicalSpace X for any given X . If you think of topologies as being primarily the data of open sets then you expect the order relation on TopologicalSpace X to come from Set (Set X) , i.e. you expect t ≤ t' if a set u is open for t' as soon as it is open for t . However we already know that Mathlib focuses on neighborhoods more than open sets so, for any x : X we want the map from topological spaces to neighborhoods fun T : TopologicalSpace X ↦ @nhds X T x to be order preserving. And we know the order relation on Filter X is designed to ensure an order preserving principal : Set X → Filter X , allowing to see filters as generalized sets. So the order relation we do use on TopologicalSpace X is opposite to the one coming from Set (Set X) .
html: Then the next big piece is a complete lattice structure on TopologicalSpace X for any given X. If you think of topologies as being primarily the data of open sets then you expect the order relation on TopologicalSpace X to come from Set (Set X), i.e. you expect t ≤ t' if a set u is open for t' as soon as it is open for t. However we already know that Mathlib focuses on neighborhoods more than open sets so, for any x : X we want the map from topological spaces to neighborhoods fun T : TopologicalSpace X ↦ @nhds X T x to be order preserving. And we know the order relation on Filter X is designed to ensure an order preserving principal : Set X → Filter X, allowing to see filters as generalized sets. So the order relation we do use on TopologicalSpace X is opposite to the one coming from Set (Set X).
zh: 下一个重要组成部分是：对任意给定的 X，TopologicalSpace X 上有完备格结构。如果把拓扑主要看作开集数据，那么会期望 TopologicalSpace X 上的序关系来自 Set (Set X)，也就是说，会期望当集合 u 对 t 开时它也对 t' 开，从而有 t ≤ t'。然而我们已经知道，Mathlib 比开集更重视邻域；所以对任意 x : X，我们希望从拓扑空间到邻域的映射 fun T : TopologicalSpace X ↦ @nhds X T x 保序。并且我们知道 Filter X 上的序关系被设计成保证 principal : Set X → Filter X 保序，从而可把滤子视为广义集合。因此，我们在 TopologicalSpace X 上使用的序关系与来自 Set (Set X) 的序关系相反。

text: Now we can recover continuity by combining the push-forward (or pull-back) operation with the order relation.
html: Now we can recover continuity by combining the push-forward (or pull-back) operation with the order relation.
zh: 现在可以把推出（或拉回）操作与序关系结合起来，重新得到连续性。

text: With this definition and the compatibility of push-forward and composition, we get for free the universal property that, for any topological space \(Z\) , a function \(g : Y → Z\) is continuous for the topology \(f_*T_X\) if and only if \(g ∘ f\) is continuous.
html: With this definition and the compatibility of push-forward and composition, we get for free the universal property that, for any topological space \(Z\), a function \(g : Y → Z\) is continuous for the topology \(f_*T_X\) if and only if \(g ∘ f\) is continuous.
zh: 借助这个定义以及推出与复合的相容性，可以免费得到如下泛性质：对任意拓扑空间 \(Z\)，函数 \(g : Y → Z\) 关于拓扑 \(f_*T_X\) 连续，当且仅当 \(g ∘ f\) 连续。

text: So we already get quotient topologies (using the projection map as f ). This wasn’t using that TopologicalSpace X is a complete lattice for all X . Let’s now see how all this structure proves the existence of the product topology by abstract non-sense. We considered the case of ℝ → ℝ above, but let’s now consider the general case of Π i, X i for some ι : Type* and X : ι → Type* . We want, for any topological space Z and any function f : Z → Π i, X i , that f is continuous if and only if (fun x ↦ x i) ∘ f is continuous for all i . Let us explore that constraint “on paper” using notation \(p_i\) for the projection (fun (x : Π i, X i) ↦ x i) :
html: So we already get quotient topologies (using the projection map as f). This wasn’t using that TopologicalSpace X is a complete lattice for all X. Let’s now see how all this structure proves the existence of the product topology by abstract non-sense. We considered the case of ℝ → ℝ above, but let’s now consider the general case of Π i, X i for some ι : Type* and X : ι → Type*. We want, for any topological space Z and any function f : Z → Π i, X i, that f is continuous if and only if (fun x ↦ x i) ∘ f is continuous for all i. Let us explore that constraint “on paper” using notation \(p_i\) for the projection (fun (x : Π i, X i) ↦ x i):
zh: 于是已经得到了商拓扑（把投影映射作为 f）。这还没有用到对所有 X，TopologicalSpace X 都是完备格。现在看看所有这些结构如何用抽象废话证明积拓扑的存在。上面考虑过 ℝ → ℝ 的情形；现在考虑一般情形：对某个 ι : Type* 和 X : ι → Type*，取 Π i, X i。我们希望对任意拓扑空间 Z 和任意函数 f : Z → Π i, X i，f 连续当且仅当对所有 i，(fun x ↦ x i) ∘ f 连续。用 \(p_i\) 表示投影 (fun (x : Π i, X i) ↦ x i)，在纸面上探索这个约束：

text: So we see that what is the topology we want on Π i, X i :
html: So we see that what is the topology we want on Π i, X i:
zh: 因此可以看出，Π i, X i 上我们想要的拓扑是什么：

text: This ends our tour of how Mathlib thinks that topological spaces fix defects of the theory of metric spaces by being a more functorial theory and having a complete lattice structure for any fixed type.
html: This ends our tour of how Mathlib thinks that topological spaces fix defects of the theory of metric spaces by being a more functorial theory and having a complete lattice structure for any fixed type.
zh: 至此，我们结束了这一巡览：Mathlib 如何通过更具函子性的理论、以及在任意固定类型上具有完备格结构，来理解拓扑空间如何修正度量空间理论的缺陷。

text: We saw that the category of topological spaces have very nice properties. The price to pay for this is existence of rather pathological topological spaces. There are a number of assumptions you can make on a topological space to ensure its behavior is closer to what metric spaces do. The most important is T2Space , also called “Hausdorff”, that will ensure that limits are unique. A stronger separation property is T3Space that ensures in addition the RegularSpace property: each point has a basis of closed neighborhoods.
html: We saw that the category of topological spaces have very nice properties. The price to pay for this is existence of rather pathological topological spaces. There are a number of assumptions you can make on a topological space to ensure its behavior is closer to what metric spaces do. The most important is T2Space, also called “Hausdorff”, that will ensure that limits are unique. A stronger separation property is T3Space that ensures in addition the RegularSpace property: each point has a basis of closed neighborhoods.
zh: 我们看到，拓扑空间范畴具有非常好的性质。代价是会出现相当病态的拓扑空间。可以对拓扑空间施加若干假设，使其行为更接近度量空间。最重要的是 T2Space，也称为“Hausdorff”，它保证极限唯一。更强的分离性质是 T3Space，它还保证 RegularSpace 性质：每个点都有由闭邻域组成的基。

text: Note that, in every topological space, each point has a basis of open neighborhood, by definition.
html: Note that, in every topological space, each point has a basis of open neighborhood, by definition.
zh: 注意，在每个拓扑空间中，每个点按定义都有一个开邻域基。

text: Our main goal is now to prove the basic theorem which allows extension by continuity. From Bourbaki’s general topology book, I.8.5, Theorem 1 (taking only the non-trivial implication):
html: Our main goal is now to prove the basic theorem which allows extension by continuity. From Bourbaki’s general topology book, I.8.5, Theorem 1 (taking only the non-trivial implication):
zh: 现在的主要目标，是证明允许由连续性作延拓的基本定理。取布尔巴基《一般拓扑》I.8.5 定理 1 中的非平凡方向：

text: Let \(X\) be a topological space, \(A\) a dense subset of \(X\) , \(f : A → Y\) a continuous mapping of \(A\) into a \(T_3\) space \(Y\) . If, for each \(x\) in \(X\) , \(f(y)\) tends to a limit in \(Y\) when \(y\) tends to \(x\) while remaining in \(A\) then there exists a continuous extension \(φ\) of \(f\) to \(X\) .
html: Let \(X\) be a topological space, \(A\) a dense subset of \(X\), \(f : A → Y\) a continuous mapping of \(A\) into a \(T_3\) space \(Y\). If, for each \(x\) in \(X\), \(f(y)\) tends to a limit in \(Y\) when \(y\) tends to \(x\) while remaining in \(A\) then there exists a continuous extension \(φ\) of \(f\) to \(X\).
zh: 设 \(X\) 为拓扑空间，\(A\) 为 \(X\) 的稠密子集，\(f : A → Y\) 为从 \(A\) 到 \(T_3\) 空间 \(Y\) 的连续映射。若对 \(X\) 中每个 \(x\)，当 \(y\) 在 \(A\) 中趋向 \(x\) 时，\(f(y)\) 在 \(Y\) 中趋向某个极限，则存在 \(f\) 到 \(X\) 的连续延拓 \(φ\)。

text: Actually Mathlib contains a more general version of the above lemma, IsDenseInducing.continuousAt_extend , but we’ll stick to Bourbaki’s version here.
html: Actually Mathlib contains a more general version of the above lemma, IsDenseInducing.continuousAt_extend, but we’ll stick to Bourbaki’s version here.
zh: 实际上，Mathlib 包含上面引理的更一般版本 IsDenseInducing.continuousAt_extend；但这里我们坚持使用布尔巴基版本。

text: Remember that, given A : Set X , ↥A is the subtype associated to A , and Lean will automatically insert that funny up arrow when needed. And the (inclusion) coercion map is (↑) : A → X . The assumption “tends to \(x\) while remaining in \(A\) ” corresponds to the pull-back filter comap (↑) (𝓝 x) .
html: Remember that, given A : Set X, ↥A is the subtype associated to A, and Lean will automatically insert that funny up arrow when needed. And the (inclusion) coercion map is (↑) : A → X. The assumption “tends to \(x\) while remaining in \(A\)” corresponds to the pull-back filter comap (↑) (𝓝 x).
zh: 请记住，给定 A : Set X，↥A 是与 A 对应的子类型；Lean 会在需要时自动插入那个特殊的上箭头。并且（包含）强制映射是 (↑) : A → X。假设“在保持于 \(A\) 中的同时趋向 \(x\)”对应于拉回滤子 comap (↑) (𝓝 x)。

text: Let’s first prove an auxiliary lemma, extracted to simplify the context (in particular we don’t need Y to be a topological space here).
html: Let’s first prove an auxiliary lemma, extracted to simplify the context (in particular we don’t need Y to be a topological space here).
zh: 先证明一个辅助引理，把它抽出是为了简化上下文（特别是这里不需要 Y 是拓扑空间）。

text: Let’s now turn to the main proof of the extension by continuity theorem.
html: Let’s now turn to the main proof of the extension by continuity theorem.
zh: 现在转向连续延拓定理的主证明。

text: When Lean needs a topology on ↥A it will automatically use the induced topology. The only relevant lemma is nhds_induced (↑) : ∀ a : ↥A, 𝓝 a = comap (↑) (𝓝 ↑a) (this is actually a general lemma about induced topologies).
html: When Lean needs a topology on ↥A it will automatically use the induced topology. The only relevant lemma is nhds_induced (↑) : ∀ a : ↥A, 𝓝 a = comap (↑) (𝓝 ↑a) (this is actually a general lemma about induced topologies).
zh: 当 Lean 需要 ↥A 上的拓扑时，它会自动使用诱导拓扑。唯一相关的引理是 nhds_induced (↑) : ∀ a : ↥A, 𝓝 a = comap (↑) (𝓝 ↑a)（这实际上是关于诱导拓扑的一般引理）。

text: The proof outline is:
html: The proof outline is:
zh: 证明提纲如下：

text: The main assumption and the axiom of choice give a function φ such that ∀ x, Tendsto f (comap (↑) (𝓝 x)) (𝓝 (φ x)) (because Y is Hausdorff, φ is entirely determined, but we won’t need that until we try to prove that φ indeed extends f ).
html: The main assumption and the axiom of choice give a function φ such that ∀ x, Tendsto f (comap (↑) (𝓝 x)) (𝓝 (φ x)) (because Y is Hausdorff, φ is entirely determined, but we won’t need that until we try to prove that φ indeed extends f).
zh: 主假设和选择公理给出一个函数 φ，使得 ∀ x, Tendsto f (comap (↑) (𝓝 x)) (𝓝 (φ x))（由于 Y 是 Hausdorff 的，φ 完全确定；但直到要证明 φ 确实延拓 f 时，才需要这一点）。

text: Let’s first prove φ is continuous. Fix any x : X . Since Y is regular, it suffices to check that for every closed neighborhood V' of φ x , φ ⁻¹' V' ∈ 𝓝 x . The limit assumption gives (through the auxiliary lemma above) some V ∈ 𝓝 x such IsOpen V ∧ (↑) ⁻¹' V ⊆ f ⁻¹' V' . Since V ∈ 𝓝 x , it suffices to prove V ⊆ φ ⁻¹' V' , i.e. ∀ y ∈ V, φ y ∈ V' . Let’s fix y in V . Because V is open , it is a neighborhood of y . In particular (↑) ⁻¹' V ∈ comap (↑) (𝓝 y) and a fortiori f ⁻¹' V' ∈ comap (↑) (𝓝 y) . In addition comap (↑) (𝓝 y) ≠ ⊥ because A is dense. Because we know Tendsto f (comap (↑) (𝓝 y)) (𝓝 (φ y)) this implies φ y ∈ closure V' and, since V' is closed, we have proved φ y ∈ V' .
html: Let’s first prove φ is continuous. Fix any x : X. Since Y is regular, it suffices to check that for every closed neighborhood V' of φ x, φ ⁻¹' V' ∈ 𝓝 x. The limit assumption gives (through the auxiliary lemma above) some V ∈ 𝓝 x such IsOpen V ∧ (↑) ⁻¹' V ⊆ f ⁻¹' V'. Since V ∈ 𝓝 x, it suffices to prove V ⊆ φ ⁻¹' V', i.e. ∀ y ∈ V, φ y ∈ V'. Let’s fix y in V. Because V is open, it is a neighborhood of y. In particular (↑) ⁻¹' V ∈ comap (↑) (𝓝 y) and a fortiori f ⁻¹' V' ∈ comap (↑) (𝓝 y). In addition comap (↑) (𝓝 y) ≠ ⊥ because A is dense. Because we know Tendsto f (comap (↑) (𝓝 y)) (𝓝 (φ y)) this implies φ y ∈ closure V' and, since V' is closed, we have proved φ y ∈ V'.
zh: 先证明 φ 连续。固定任意 x : X。由于 Y 是正则的，只需检查对 φ x 的每个闭邻域 V'，都有 φ ⁻¹' V' ∈ 𝓝 x。极限假设通过上面的辅助引理给出某个 V ∈ 𝓝 x，满足 IsOpen V ∧ (↑) ⁻¹' V ⊆ f ⁻¹' V'。由于 V ∈ 𝓝 x，只需证明 V ⊆ φ ⁻¹' V'，即 ∀ y ∈ V, φ y ∈ V'。固定 V 中的 y。因为 V 是开的，它是 y 的邻域。特别地，(↑) ⁻¹' V ∈ comap (↑) (𝓝 y)；进而有 f ⁻¹' V' ∈ comap (↑) (𝓝 y)。此外，由于 A 稠密，comap (↑) (𝓝 y) ≠ ⊥。因为已知 Tendsto f (comap (↑) (𝓝 y)) (𝓝 (φ y))，这推出 φ y ∈ closure V'；又因为 V' 是闭的，便证明了 φ y ∈ V'。

text: It remains to prove that φ extends f . This is where the continuity of f enters the discussion, together with the fact that Y is Hausdorff.
html: It remains to prove that φ extends f. This is where the continuity of f enters the discussion, together with the fact that Y is Hausdorff.
zh: 剩下的是证明 φ 延拓 f。这一步中会用到 f 的连续性，以及 Y 是 Hausdorff 的事实。

text: In addition to separation property, the main kind of assumption you can make on a topological space to bring it closer to metric spaces is countability assumption. The main one is first countability asking that every point has a countable neighborhood basis. In particular this ensures that closure of sets can be understood using sequences.
html: In addition to separation property, the main kind of assumption you can make on a topological space to bring it closer to metric spaces is countability assumption. The main one is first countability asking that every point has a countable neighborhood basis. In particular this ensures that closure of sets can be understood using sequences.
zh: 除了分离性之外，使拓扑空间更接近度量空间的主要假设类型是可数性假设。其中最主要的是第一可数性，它要求每个点都有一个可数邻域基。特别地，这保证可以用序列来理解集合闭包。

text: Let us now discuss how compactness is defined for topological spaces. As usual there are several ways to think about it and Mathlib goes for the filter version.
html: Let us now discuss how compactness is defined for topological spaces. As usual there are several ways to think about it and Mathlib goes for the filter version.
zh: 现在讨论拓扑空间中的紧性如何定义。照常，有多种理解方式，而 Mathlib 采用滤子版本。

text: We first need to define cluster points of filters. Given a filter F on a topological space X , a point x : X is a cluster point of F if F , seen as a generalized set, has non-empty intersection with the generalized set of points that are close to x .
html: We first need to define cluster points of filters. Given a filter F on a topological space X, a point x : X is a cluster point of F if F, seen as a generalized set, has non-empty intersection with the generalized set of points that are close to x.
zh: 首先需要定义滤子的聚点。给定拓扑空间 X 上的滤子 F，点 x : X 称为 F 的聚点，当且仅当把 F 视为广义集合时，它与接近 x 的点组成的广义集合有非空交。

text: Then we can say that a set s is compact if every nonempty generalized set F contained in s , i.e. such that F ≤ 𝓟 s , has a cluster point in s .
html: Then we can say that a set s is compact if every nonempty generalized set F contained in s, i.e. such that F ≤ 𝓟 s, has a cluster point in s.
zh: 于是可以说，集合 s 是紧的，当且仅当每个包含在 s 中的非空广义集合 F，即每个满足 F ≤ 𝓟 s 的滤子，都在 s 中有聚点。

text: For instance if F is map u atTop , the image under u : ℕ → X of atTop , the generalized set of very large natural numbers, then the assumption F ≤ 𝓟 s means that u n belongs to s for n large enough. Saying that x is a cluster point of map u atTop says the image of very large numbers intersects the set of points that are close to x . In case 𝓝 x has a countable basis, we can interpret this as saying that u has a subsequence converging to x , and we get back what compactness looks like in metric spaces.
html: For instance if F is map u atTop, the image under u : ℕ → X of atTop, the generalized set of very large natural numbers, then the assumption F ≤ 𝓟 s means that u n belongs to s for n large enough. Saying that x is a cluster point of map u atTop says the image of very large numbers intersects the set of points that are close to x. In case 𝓝 x has a countable basis, we can interpret this as saying that u has a subsequence converging to x, and we get back what compactness looks like in metric spaces.
zh: 例如，若 F 是 map u atTop，即 atTop 这个非常大的自然数组成的广义集合在 u : ℕ → X 下的像，则假设 F ≤ 𝓟 s 意味着对充分大的 n，u n 属于 s。说 x 是 map u atTop 的聚点，就是说非常大自然数的像与接近 x 的点的集合相交。当 𝓝 x 有可数基时，可以把它解释为：u 有一个收敛到 x 的子序列；这就恢复了度量空间中紧性的样子。

text: Cluster points behave nicely with continuous functions.
html: Cluster points behave nicely with continuous functions.
zh: 聚点在连续函数下表现良好。

text: As an exercise, we will prove that the image of a compact set under a continuous map is compact. In addition to what we saw already, you should use Filter.push_pull and NeBot.of_map .
html: As an exercise, we will prove that the image of a compact set under a continuous map is compact. In addition to what we saw already, you should use Filter.push_pull and NeBot.of_map.
zh: 作为练习，我们将证明紧集在连续映射下的像是紧的。除了已经见过的内容之外，还应使用 Filter.push_pull 和 NeBot.of_map。

text: One can also express compactness in terms of open covers: s is compact if every family of open sets that cover s has a finite covering sub-family.
html: One can also express compactness in terms of open covers: s is compact if every family of open sets that cover s has a finite covering sub-family.
zh: 也可以用开覆盖来表达紧性：s 是紧的，当且仅当每个覆盖 s 的开集族都有一个有限子族仍然覆盖它。

text: limits , including all the kinds of limits discussed above: finite and infinite limits of sequences, finite and infinite limits of functions at a point or at infinity, and so on.
html: • limits, including all the kinds of limits discussed above: finite and infinite limits of sequences, finite and infinite limits of functions at a point or at infinity, and so on.
zh: 极限，包括上文讨论的所有极限类型：序列的有限与无限极限，函数在一点或无穷远处的有限与无限极限，等等。

text: things happening eventually , including things happening for large enough n : ℕ , or sufficiently near a point x , or for sufficiently close pairs of points, or almost everywhere in the sense of measure theory. Dually, filters can also express the idea of things happening often : for arbitrarily large n , at a point in any neighborhood of a given point, etc.
html: • things happening eventually, including things happening for large enough n : ℕ, or sufficiently near a point x, or for sufficiently close pairs of points, or almost everywhere in the sense of measure theory. Dually, filters can also express the idea of things happening often: for arbitrarily large n, at a point in any neighborhood of a given point, etc.
zh: 最终成立的性质，包括对充分大的 n : ℕ 成立、在某点 x 的足够近处成立、对足够接近的点对成立，或在测度论意义下几乎处处成立。对偶地，滤子也可以表达频繁成立的性质：对任意大的 n、在给定点任意邻域中的某一点，等等。

text: (atTop : Filter ℕ) , made of sets of ℕ containing {n | n ≥ N} for some N
html: • (atTop : Filter ℕ), made of sets of ℕ containing {n | n ≥ N} for some N
zh: (atTop : Filter ℕ)，由那些包含某个 N 对应的集合 {n | n ≥ N} 的 ℕ 的子集组成

text: 𝓝 x , made of neighborhoods of x in a topological space
html: • 𝓝 x, made of neighborhoods of x in a topological space
zh: 𝓝 x，由拓扑空间中 x 的邻域组成

text: 𝓤 X , made of entourages of a uniform space (uniform spaces generalize metric spaces and topological groups)
html: • 𝓤 X, made of entourages of a uniform space (uniform spaces generalize metric spaces and topological groups)
zh: 𝓤 X，由一致空间的 entourage 组成（一致空间推广了度量空间和拓扑群）

text: μ.ae , made of sets whose complement has zero measure with respect to a measure μ .
html: • μ.ae , made of sets whose complement has zero measure with respect to a measure μ.
zh: μ.ae，由相对于测度 μ 其补集测度为零的集合组成。

text: F.univ_sets : univ ∈ F.sets
html: • F.univ_sets : univ ∈ F.sets
zh: 条件 F.univ_sets : univ ∈ F.sets

text: F.sets_of_superset : ∀ {U V}, U ∈ F.sets → U ⊆ V → V ∈ F.sets
html: • F.sets_of_superset : ∀ {U V}, U ∈ F.sets → U ⊆ V → V ∈ F.sets
zh: 条件 F.sets_of_superset : ∀ {U V}, U ∈ F.sets → U ⊆ V → V ∈ F.sets

text: F.inter_sets : ∀ {U V}, U ∈ F.sets → V ∈ F.sets → U ∩ V ∈ F.sets .
html: • F.inter_sets : ∀ {U V}, U ∈ F.sets → V ∈ F.sets → U ∩ V ∈ F.sets.
zh: 条件 F.inter_sets : ∀ {U V}, U ∈ F.sets → V ∈ F.sets → U ∩ V ∈ F.sets。

text: the condition univ ∈ F ensures (∀ x, P x) → ∀ᶠ x in F, P x ,
html: • the condition univ ∈ F ensures (∀ x, P x) → ∀ᶠ x in F, P x,
zh: 条件 univ ∈ F 保证 (∀ x, P x) → ∀ᶠ x in F, P x，

text: the condition U ∈ F → U ⊆ V → V ∈ F ensures (∀ᶠ x in F, P x) → (∀ x, P x → Q x) → ∀ᶠ x in F, Q x , and
html: • the condition U ∈ F → U ⊆ V → V ∈ F ensures (∀ᶠ x in F, P x) → (∀ x, P x → Q x) → ∀ᶠ x in F, Q x, and
zh: 条件 U ∈ F → U ⊆ V → V ∈ F 保证 (∀ᶠ x in F, P x) → (∀ x, P x → Q x) → ∀ᶠ x in F, Q x，并且

text: the condition U ∈ F → V ∈ F → U ∩ V ∈ F ensures (∀ᶠ x in F, P x) → (∀ᶠ x in F, Q x) → ∀ᶠ x in F, P x ∧ Q x .
html: • the condition U ∈ F → V ∈ F → U ∩ V ∈ F ensures (∀ᶠ x in F, P x) → (∀ᶠ x in F, Q x) → ∀ᶠ x in F, P x ∧ Q x.
zh: 条件 U ∈ F → V ∈ F → U ∩ V ∈ F 保证 (∀ᶠ x in F, P x) → (∀ᶠ x in F, Q x) → ∀ᶠ x in F, P x ∧ Q x。

text: Any sequence with values in a compact set has a subsequence that converges in this set.
html: • Any sequence with values in a compact set has a subsequence that converges in this set.
zh: 任意取值于紧集的序列都有一个在该集合中收敛的子序列。

text: Any continuous function on a nonempty compact set with values in real numbers is bounded and attains its bounds somewhere (this is called the extreme value theorem).
html: • Any continuous function on a nonempty compact set with values in real numbers is bounded and attains its bounds somewhere (this is called the extreme value theorem).
zh: 任意定义在非空紧集上且取实数值的连续函数都是有界的，并且在某处达到其界（这称为极值定理）。

text: Compact sets are closed sets.
html: • Compact sets are closed sets.
zh: 紧集是闭集。
